jobs.title: Senior Software Development Engineer - Frontend
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Filevine is a Legal AI company delivering Legal Operating Intelligence for the future of legal work. Grounded in a singular system of truth, Filevine brings together data, documents, workflows, and teams into one unified platform—where modern legal work happens with clarity and consistency.

Powered by LOIS, the Legal Operating Intelligence System, Filevine connects context across every matter to transform legal operations from reactive to proactive. LOIS reads, understands, and reasons across your data to surface insight, automate complexity, and give professionals the clarity and confidence to see more, know more, and do more. Fueled by a team of exceptional collaborators and innovators, Filevine’s rapid growth has earned AI awards and recognition from Deloitte and Inc. as one of the most innovative and fastest-growing technology companies in the country.

Role Summary:

We’re seeking a mid-to-senior Frontend Engineer to help lead the development of a new greenfield application, working closely with engineering and QA to deliver high-quality, scalable features. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in autonomous environments, collaborates well across teams, and brings a strong focus on performance, usability, and modern frontend best practices.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the architecture and innovation of the frontend, designing sophisticated, reusable component libraries and UI patterns in Svelte
  • Build responsive, intuitive interfaces tailored to complex legal workflows, including real-time collaboration, data visualization, and AI-powered features (e.g., document summaries, case insights)
  • Optimize frontend performance and scalability to handle large datasets such as case files, timelines, and analytics dashboards
  • Implement best-in-class UI/UX practices, ensuring full WCAG accessibility compliance, cross-browser compatibility, and seamless mobile responsiveness
  • Own the complete lifecycle of major features—from gathering requirements and prototyping through implementation, testing, CI/CD deployment, and post-launch monitoring
  • Enforce engineering excellence by conducting thorough code reviews, writing comprehensive tests (unit, integration, end-to-end), and maintaining strong observability
  • Mentor mid-level and junior frontend engineers in modern JavaScript and/or Svelte best practices, component-driven development, and clean, scalable UI architecture
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, UX designers, backend engineers, and legal domain experts to translate complex user needs into elegant, high-impact solutions
  • Actively participate in cross-team planning, design reviews, sprint ceremonies, and frontend standards discussions
  • Communicate technical trade-offs, UI decisions, and user impact clearly to both technical teams and non-technical stakeholders, while helping shape the frontend roadmap and standards




Qualifications
  • 6+ years of professional frontend development experience with expert-level mastery of JavaScript, TypeScript, and Svelte
  • Proven production experience building complex, data-intensive single-page applications (SPAs) that handle large datasets and real-time interactions
  • Strong, demonstrable portfolio showcasing exceptional UI/UX design, responsive layouts, high performance optimization, and full accessibility compliance (e.g., WCAG)
  • Solid understanding of backend integration, with bonus preference for experience consuming NET / C# APIs
  • Exposure to AI-enhanced user interfaces, intelligent features (e.g., summaries, insights), or tools built for legal, technical, or workflow-heavy domains
  • Prior work in regulated industries or applications managing high-stakes, sensitive data is highly valued
  • Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate UI decisions, user flows, and technical trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders and cross-functional partners
  • Growth mindset, strong collaborative approach, genuine passion for mentoring others, and proven track record of independently owning and delivering high-impact features under tight, ambitious timelines
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field preferred—equivalent experience, certifications, or demonstrated expertise with a strong production track record considered equally




What's In It For You
  • Tackle meaningful challenges in legal tech with direct impact on thousands of users
  • Work with a modern, evolving frontend stack (Svelte-focused) alongside a robust .NET backend
  • Autonomy and ownership—lead UI initiatives from concept to production
  • Collaborative, supportive culture with regular learning opportunities and architecture discussions
  • Competitive salary, equity, full health benefits, generous PTO, and company-wide recharge days




Compensation Information: $160,000 - $200,000

The base salary range represents the low and high end of the salary range for this position. The total compensation package for this position will be determined by each individual’s location, qualifications, education, work experience, skills and performance. We believe in the importance of pay equity - the range listed is just one component of Filevine’s total compensation package for employees. This position is also eligible for a paid time off policy, as well as a comprehensive benefits package.

Cool Company Benefits:
  • A dynamic, rapidly growing company, focused on helping organizations thrive
  • Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance (for full-time employees)
  • Competitive & Fair Pay
  • Maternity & paternity leave (for full-time employees)
  • Short & long-term disability
  • Opportunity to learn from a dedicated leadership team
  • Top-of-the-line company swag




Privacy Policy Notice
Filevine will handle your personal information according to what’s outlined in our Privacy Policy.

Communication about this opportunity, or any open role at Filevine, will only come from representatives with email addresses using "filevine.com". Other addresses reaching out are not affiliated with Filevine and should not be responded to.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions

  • Mentor mid-level and junior frontend engineers in modern JavaScript and/or Svelte best practices, component-driven development, and clean, scalable UI architecture
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, UX designers, backend engineers, and legal domain experts to translate complex user needs into elegant, high-impact solutions
  • Actively participate in cross-team planning, design reviews, sprint ceremonies, and frontend standards discussions
  • Communicate technical trade-offs, UI decisions, and user impact clearly to both technical teams and non-technical stakeholders, while helping shape the frontend roadmap and standards




Qualifications
  • 6+ years of professional frontend development experience with expert-level mastery of JavaScript, TypeScript, and Svelte
  • Proven production experience building complex, data-intensive single-page applications (SPAs) that handle large datasets and real-time interactions
  • Strong, demonstrable portfolio showcasing exceptional UI/UX design, responsive layouts, high performance optimization, and full accessibility compliance (e.g., WCAG)
  • Solid understanding of backend integration, with bonus preference for experience consuming NET / C# APIs
  • Exposure to AI-enhanced user interfaces, intelligent features (e.g., summaries, insights), or tools built for legal, technical, or workflow-heavy domains
  • Prior work in regulated industries or applications managing high-stakes, sensitive data is highly valued
  • Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate UI decisions, user flows, and technical trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders and cross-functional partners
  • Growth mindset, strong collaborative approach, genuine passion for mentoring others, and proven track record of independently owning and delivering high-impact features under tight, ambitious timelines
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field preferred—equivalent experience, certifications, or demonstrated expertise with a strong production track record considered equally




What's In It For You
  • Tackle meaningful challenges in legal tech with direct impact on thousands of users
  • Work with a modern, evolving frontend stack (Svelte-focused) alongside a robust .NET backend
  • Autonomy and ownership—lead UI initiatives from concept to production
  • Collaborative, supportive culture with regular learning opportunities and architecture discussions
  • Competitive salary, equity, full health benefits, generous PTO, and company-wide recharge days




Compensation Information: $160,000 - $200,000

The base salary range represents the low and high end of the salary range for this position. The total compensation package for this position will be determined by each individual’s location, qualifications, education, work experience, skills and performance. We believe in the importance of pay equity - the range listed is just one component of Filevine’s total compensation package for employees. This position is also eligible for a paid time off policy, as well as a comprehensive benefits package.

Cool Company Benefits:
  • A dynamic, rapidly growing company, focused on helping organizations thrive
  • Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance (for full-time employees)
  • Competitive & Fair Pay
  • Maternity & paternity leave (for full-time employees)
  • Short & long-term disability
  • Opportunity to learn from a dedicated leadership team
  • Top-of-the-line company swag




Privacy Policy Notice
Filevine will handle your personal information according to what’s outlined in our Privacy Policy.

Communication about this opportunity, or any open role at Filevine, will only come from representatives with email addresses using "filevine.com". Other addresses reaching out are not affiliated with Filevine and should not be responded to.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions

jobs.title: Senior React Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Job title: Senior React Developer

Location: Remote

Employment Type: Full-time (W2 only, No C2C)

Experience: 10+ Years

Job Summary: We are seeking a highly experienced React Developer with 10+ years of professional experience in designing and developing modern, scalable, and high-performance web applications. The ideal candidate will possess deep expertise in React.js, JavaScript/TypeScript, front-end architecture, UI component design, state management, and API integration. The candidate will work closely with UI/UX designers, backend engineers, and DevOps teams to build responsive and user-centric applications while following modern Agile and DevOps practices.

Key Responsibilities:
  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable single-page applications (SPA) using React.js, JavaScript (ES6+), and TypeScript, ensuring high performance, maintainability, and reusable component architecture.
  • Build and maintain reusable UI components and front-end libraries using React Hooks, Functional Components, JSX, and modern front-end development practices.
  • Implement state management solutions using Redux, Redux Toolkit, Context API, or MobX to manage complex application state across large-scale enterprise applications.
  • Develop responsive and mobile-first web interfaces using HTML5, CSS3, SASS, Bootstrap, Tailwind CSS, and Material UI, ensuring cross-browser compatibility and accessibility standards.
  • Integrate front-end applications with RESTful APIs and microservices using Axios, Fetch API, or GraphQL, enabling seamless data communication between front-end and backend systems.
  • Optimize application performance using React performance techniques such as code splitting, lazy loading, memoization, virtual DOM optimization, and server-side rendering (SSR).
  • Implement unit testing and component testing using Jest, React Testing Library, Enzyme, or Cypress, ensuring code reliability and application stability.
  • Collaborate with UI/UX designers to translate wireframes and prototypes into interactive and visually appealing web interfaces.
  • Integrate applications with CI/CD pipelines using tools such as Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Azure DevOps to enable automated build, test, and deployment processes.
  • Utilize version control systems such as Git, GitHub, Bitbucket, or GitLab to manage source code and collaborate with distributed development teams.
  • Implement modern front-end architecture patterns such as Component-Based Architecture, Micro Frontends, and Modular Development.
  • Work within Agile/Scrum environments, participating in sprint planning, code reviews, and continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Ensure application security, scalability, and maintainability by following secure coding practices, coding standards, and front-end best practices.
  • Mentor junior developers, perform code reviews, and contribute to improving development standards and best practices across the engineering team.

Technical Skills:
  • Front-End Framework: React.js
  • Programming Languages: JavaScript (ES6+), TypeScript
  • State Management: Redux, Redux Toolkit, Context API, MobX
  • UI Technologies: HTML5, CSS3, SASS, LESS
  • UI Libraries & Frameworks: Material UI, Bootstrap, Tailwind CSS, Ant Design
  • API Integration: REST APIs, GraphQL, Axios, Fetch API
  • Testing Frameworks: Jest, React Testing Library, Enzyme, Cypress
  • Build Tools: Webpack, Babel, Vite, npm, Yarn
  • Version Control: Git, GitHub, Bitbucket, GitLab
  • CI/CD Tools: Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps
  • Cloud Platforms: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud Platform
  • Containerization: Docker, Kubernetes
  • Methodologies: Agile, Scrum, DevOps
  • Operating Systems: Windows, Linux, macOS

jobs.title: Web Frontend Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Company Overview

United Parks & Resorts Inc. (NYSE: PRKS) is a leading theme park and entertainment company with a world-class portfolio of seven brands across 13 parks in the United States and Abu Dhabi. The Company’s award-winning portfolio of iconic brands includes SeaWorld, Busch Gardens, Discovery Cove, Sesame Place, Water Country USA, Adventure Island, and Aquatica.


Role Overview

United Parks & Resorts is hiring a Front-End Engineer to build modern Web experiences using React, powered extensively by Sitecore CMS. You’ll deliver fast, accessible, mobile-first UI that is authorable, resilient to real-world content variation, and built with strong automated test coverage. This position is eligible for Remote Work.


Key Responsibilities
  • Build React UI components integrated with Sitecore CMS (authorable, reusable, resilient)
  • Implement mobile-first responsive UI and ensure strong accessibility (WCAG-minded)
  • Optimize performance and stability (Core Web Vitals: LCP/CLS/INP)
  • Integrate with APIs and handle loading/error/empty states cleanly
  • Support controlled rollouts and A/B testing / experimentation in a clean, performance-safe way (Optimizely experience is a plus)
  • Own quality end-to-end through automated testing and the right level of code coverage
  • Use modern AI-assisted development tools responsibly to improve productivity, code quality, and developer velocity


Qualifications
  • 4+ years building production Web apps with React, TypeScript, HTML, CSS
  • Solid experience working with Sitecore, Contentful, or other headless/hybrid CMS
  • Mobile-first mindset: Touch-friendly UI patterns, responsive images, performance on slower devices/networks
  • Expertise in unit and integration testing using Jest, React Testing Library, Cucumber, or other testing frameworks/libraries
  • Experience building customer-facing E-Commerce journeys such as product discovery/browse, search, product detail pages, cart and checkout flows, promotions/discounts, and conversion-focused UX
  • Understanding of CDN/edge delivery behavior (caching/performance impact on UI delivery)
  • Experience with cloud native development using Azure, AWS, or GCP

jobs.title: Angular Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Remote within the U.S. only. All hires will be W-2 employees paid through U.S. payroll. We do not accept applications from third-party recruiters or staffing agencies.


ProSearch is hiring a remote Senior Angular Engineer (Angular 20) for a long-term contract opportunity supporting a confidential enterprise client’s Digital Communications Platform.

This enterprise platform powers customer communications across email, SMS, portal notifications, and print. The team is backend-heavy today, primarily Python in AWS, and is now investing significantly in elevating the frontend experience. This hire will serve as the primary UI engineer within a pod alongside two backend engineers.

If you are a senior-level frontend engineer who values ownership, collaboration, and enterprise-scale impact, this role delivers all three.

A Collaborative Remote Culture

This is not an isolated contract role. Although fully remote within the United States, the team operates with consistent touch points, active communication channels, and strong cross-functional engagement. You will collaborate daily with backend engineers, UX, and Product partners.

Core working hours are 11:00 AM to 7:00 PM EST to support real-time interaction. Engineers are expected to be accessible via team communication tools and available to connect when needed. The culture emphasizes shared ownership, transparency, and partnership, not solo contribution in a vacuum.

What You Will Do

This role is 99% frontend focused using Angular 20.

You will:
  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable enterprise Angular applications
  • Own the full frontend lifecycle from implementation through automated testing
  • Build communication preference management interfaces
  • Develop notification inbox and messaging experiences
  • Support template rendering services and future digital communication capabilities
  • Implement end-to-end browser testing using Cypress
  • Maintain unit test coverage using Jest
  • Translate business and UX requirements into intuitive, production-ready UI solutions
  • Contribute thoughtful enhancements within established frontend architecture and design standards
  • There is no dedicated QA resource, so frontend quality and automated testing ownership are essential.

Required Qualifications
  • 5+ years of professional front-end engineering experience, with at least 3 years working in Angular (v2+)
  • Strong command of TypeScript, HTML5, CSS3/SCSS, and modern JavaScript (ES6+)
  • Senior-level experience building production-grade applications with Angular 20
  • Strong enterprise frontend development experience
  • Experience operating within defined UI patterns and architectural standards
  • Hands-on experience with Cypress and Jest
  • Ability to execute independently while remaining highly collaborative
  • Strong communication skills in distributed remote teams
  • Availability during EST core hours

Preferred Experience
  • Exposure to AWS environments
  • Familiarity with Python-based backend ecosystems
  • Experience building notification systems, inbox interfaces, preference centers, or template management tools
  • Experience leveraging AI-assisted development tools

Why This Opportunity
  • Fully remote within the United States
  • 1+ year contract with strong potential for extension
  • High-visibility enterprise platform
  • Clear long-term investment in digital communications
  • Autonomy balanced with consistent team collaboration

This is an opportunity to shape the front-end experience of a growing enterprise communications platform while working in a connected, engaged, and highly collaborative remote team.


Ready to Elevate Enterprise Frontend?

If you are a Senior Angular Engineer seeking a remote contract role in the United States where you can own enterprise UI development without working in isolation, we encourage you to apply.


Application Process:

This role is open exclusively to candidates applying directly. Submissions from recruiters, agencies, or other third-party sources will not be considered. By submitting an application, you confirm you are applying directly and not through a third-party recruiter. ProSearch will not be responsible for any fees or claims related to unsolicited third-party submissions.

jobs.title: Frontend Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Title: Senior Frontend Engineer (Early Stage AI Startup )

Location: San Fransisco - Onsite/Hybrid

Compensation: $150,000 – $225,000 + Equity


The Opportunity An elite LegalTech start-up on a mission to transform the $100B+ immigration industry. By merging the expertise of seasoned attorneys with cutting-edge AI/ML, we are streamlining complex legal workflows for individuals and global businesses.


Currently, they're scaling the engineering team from 5 to 15+ to meet their ambitious 2026 roadmap, to increase their current valuation of Over $100m+


What You’ll Do
  • Own the technical design and implementation of our core product using React and Next.js.
  • Translate complex product designs into high-performance, scalable technical architectures.
  • Work directly with the CTO and CPO to define the user experience for an AI-native legal platform.
  • Solve "unsolved" challenges in legal data visualization and automated workflow interfaces.


Who You Are
  • Experience: 5+ years of Frontend Engineering experience, ideally within a high-growth, VC-backed startup environment.
  • Stack: Mastery of React and Next.js is non-negotiable.
  • Pedigree: Degree from a top-tier CS program or a track record of excellence at reputable, high-bar engineering companies.
  • Mindset: You thrive in high-intensity environments and prefer rapid execution over corporate bureaucracy.


The Perks
  • Top-of-market salary and significant equity.
  • Full Visa Sponsorship available (H1-B, O-1, etc.).
  • The chance to be a foundational member of a future unicorn.

jobs.title: Senior Vue.js Developer - 100% Remote
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Optomi, in partnership with a leading retail and wholesale holding company, is seeking a Senior Frontend Developer to join their growing team! This role focuses on building and maintaining enterprise applications using VueJS, Pinia/Vuex, Tailwind, and Nuxt, with a strong emphasis on testing (Jest, unit testing) and CI/CD. It’s a hands-on product engineering position centered on performance, scalability, and clean architecture.


Responsibilities:
  • Design front-end and server-side solutions.
  • Write APIs for our consumer-facing flows, whether for web or mobile.
  • Test software to ensure software responsiveness and efficiency.
  • Troubleshoot, debug and upgrade software.
  • Write technical documentation.
  • Work with implementation teams to automate and ensure standard methodologies on infrastructure deployments.
  • Deliver solutions and systems: consistent, optimized, resilient, documented, secure.


Experience:

  • Bachelor's Degree or equivalent professional experience.
  • 5+ years of experience building and maintaining complex enterprise applications.
  • Possess expertise in FE frameworks including, but not limited to: VueJS, Pinia/Vuex, TailWind CSS, Nuxt, Webpack, Yarn, ESLint & SonarLint, Jest, Vue Test Utils.
  • Excellent knowledge of Continuous Delivery process and tools, testing frameworks and practices.
  • Understand the testing pyramid and develop suite of unit tests, API tests and adopts "test-as-you-code" strategy.

jobs.title: Web User Interface Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

We are looking for a talented JavaScript / React UI Developer to join a fast-paced engineering team building modern, highly scalable web applications. This role focuses heavily on building intuitive user interfaces, collaborating with product and backend teams, and delivering high-quality, responsive experiences for end users.


This is a great opportunity for a developer who enjoys working with modern front-end frameworks and contributing to clean, maintainable UI architecture.


Responsibilities
  • Develop and maintain modern web applications using JavaScript (ES6+) and React.js
  • Build responsive, high-performance user interfaces and reusable UI components
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to translate requirements into functional UI features
  • Optimize applications for maximum speed, scalability, and performance
  • Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code
  • Participate in code reviews and contribute to best practices across the front-end team
  • Integrate RESTful APIs and backend services into the UI layer
  • Troubleshoot and resolve front-end issues across browsers and devices


Required Qualifications
  • Strong experience with JavaScript and React.js
  • Experience building modern, component-based front-end architectures
  • Solid understanding of HTML5, CSS3, and responsive design
  • Experience working with REST APIs
  • Familiarity with modern development tools such as Git, npm/yarn, and modern build tools
  • Strong problem-solving and debugging skills
  • Ability to work collaboratively in an agile development environment





Interview Process

The interview process will begin with a recorded technical interview consisting of a question-and-answer discussion along with a coding exercise completed for the client.

Candidates who successfully complete this stage will move forward to a face-to-face interview with the engineering team, where they will discuss technical experience, architecture decisions, and team collaboration.

jobs.title: Web User Interface Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

We are looking for a talented JavaScript / React UI Developer to join a fast-paced engineering team building modern, highly scalable web applications. This role focuses heavily on building intuitive user interfaces, collaborating with product and backend teams, and delivering high-quality, responsive experiences for end users.


This is a great opportunity for a developer who enjoys working with modern front-end frameworks and contributing to clean, maintainable UI architecture.


Responsibilities
  • Develop and maintain modern web applications using JavaScript (ES6+) and React.js
  • Build responsive, high-performance user interfaces and reusable UI components
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to translate requirements into functional UI features
  • Optimize applications for maximum speed, scalability, and performance
  • Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code
  • Participate in code reviews and contribute to best practices across the front-end team
  • Integrate RESTful APIs and backend services into the UI layer
  • Troubleshoot and resolve front-end issues across browsers and devices


Required Qualifications
  • Strong experience with JavaScript and React.js
  • Experience building modern, component-based front-end architectures
  • Solid understanding of HTML5, CSS3, and responsive design
  • Experience working with REST APIs
  • Familiarity with modern development tools such as Git, npm/yarn, and modern build tools
  • Strong problem-solving and debugging skills
  • Ability to work collaboratively in an agile development environment





Interview Process

The interview process will begin with a recorded technical interview consisting of a question-and-answer discussion along with a coding exercise completed for the client.

Candidates who successfully complete this stage will move forward to a face-to-face interview with the engineering team, where they will discuss technical experience, architecture decisions, and team collaboration.

jobs.title: Senior Front End Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Company Overview


Our client is a new, fully remote market maker on prediction markets, funded by a tier 1 operator to build next-generation trading systems, leveraging huge industry IP & resources from the tier 1 operator. The founding team includes true industry veterans with a vision for the future. They are building a lean, fully remote, senior team focused on high-performance systems, pragmatic engineering, and rapid iteration.


Mission: Build fast, ergonomic internal trading tools that give engineers and traders real-time visibility into exchange activity and the ability to interact directly with our systems — from monitoring live order flow to operational controls and order book interaction.


What You Will Do


• Lead the development of performant, real-time trader-facing UIs: blotters, order tickets, risk views, dashboards, and order book displays.

• Build interactive operational tooling that allows engineers and traders to act on what they see — suspending or enabling trading activity, sending commands to the system, managing positions, and interacting with live order books.

• Design and implement lightweight backend services (e.g., REST APIs, WebSocket servers) that aggregate, transform, and serve data to your front-ends — full ownership from data feed to UI.

• Shape the front-end stack and standards for state management, testing, and performance.

• Design for real-time data over WebSockets/streaming; ensure graceful degradation and resilience.

• Work directly with trading and backend engineers to understand workflow requirements and translate them into intuitive, high-performance interfaces — there are no designers or product managers; good product instincts are part of the role.

• Build reusable component libraries and a lightweight design system tailored to trading workflows.

• Instrument apps for telemetry and user-centric performance (TTI, frame times), and partner on end-to-end observability.

• Maintain high code quality through reviews, automation, and thoughtful documentation.

What You Bring

• 5+ years building complex front-end applications, ideally in real-time or data-heavy domains.

• Expertise with a modern framework (React is the current stack) and TypeScript.

• Strong grasp of state management (Redux, Zustand, MobX, or similar) and testing (Jest, React Testing Library).

• Experience with real-time transports (WebSocket, SSE) and data visualization (e.g., Highcharts, D3, Recharts).

• Comfort building and owning lightweight backend services to support your UIs — Python/FastAPI or similar; this is not a pure front-end role.

• Keen product/UX instincts and a pragmatic approach to trade-offs; able to own UX decisions without dedicated design support.

• A genuine enthusiasm for AI-assisted development. You are actively using AI coding tools in your daily workflow, you experiment on your own, and you bring that curiosity to how you approach problems. AI proficiency is not about a specific tool — it's about having the mindset and habit of leveraging AI as a force multiplier.


Domain Knowledge:


Our ideal candidate brings meaningful familiarity with one of the following:


• Sports betting — You understand markets, odds, order flow, or have worked on trading, risk, or operations tooling in this space.

• Financial markets or prediction markets — Exchange, derivatives, or prediction market background with a demonstrated interest in applying that to sports.

• Strong independent interest — Meaningful side projects or self-directed learning in sports betting, financial exchanges, or prediction markets are equally welcome.



Nice to Have


• Experience with AG Grid or similar enterprise data grid libraries; high-density, real-time tabular data is central to our UIs.

• Familiarity with order book visualization and the mechanics of exchange-traded markets.

• Exposure to Electron or desktop packaging for internal tools.

jobs.title: React Native Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Job Title: React Native Mobile Developer

Location: 100% Remote

Employment Type: Contract

Work Authorization: Candidates must be authorized to work in the U.S. without current or future sponsorship requirements.


Job Description

We are seeking an experienced React Native Mobile Developer to support the development and enhancement of the orchestration layer for a proprietary mobile application. The ideal candidate will have strong experience building cross-platform mobile applications using React Native and working with Bluetooth-enabled device integrations.

This role involves collaborating with engineering teams to build scalable mobile solutions, integrate device communication capabilities, and ensure seamless interaction between application layers.

Key Responsibilities
  • Design, develop, and maintain cross-platform mobile applications using React Native.
  • Contribute to building and enhancing the orchestration layer for a proprietary mobile platform.
  • Implement and support Bluetooth connectivity and device integration within the mobile application.
  • Work closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver high-quality mobile solutions.
  • Troubleshoot, debug, and optimize mobile application performance.
  • Follow best practices for mobile development, testing, and deployment.

Required Skills & Qualifications
  • Strong experience with React Native mobile development.
  • Experience building mobile applications for iOS and Android.
  • Hands-on experience with Bluetooth integration in mobile applications.
  • Proficiency in JavaScript/TypeScript.
  • Experience with mobile APIs, state management, and modern mobile architecture patterns.
  • Strong problem-solving and collaboration skills.




Regards,

Purnima Pobbathy

Senior Technical Recruiter

972 737 8626

| purnima@themesoft.com |Themesoft Inc |

jobs.title: Senior Front End UI Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Apogee Global RMS has a client who is seeking a Front‑End & UI Developer who can build intuitive, high‑performance user interfaces for modern web applications. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast‑moving startup environment, brings strong design sensibility, and can translate complex requirements into clean, responsive UI experiences.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain front‑end features using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frameworks (React, Angular, or Vue)
  • Transform UI/UX wireframes into functional, pixel‑perfect interfaces
  • Optimize applications for speed, scalability, and cross‑browser compatibility
  • Collaborate with product, design, and backend teams to deliver seamless user experiences
  • Implement reusable components, design systems, and UI standards
  • Troubleshoot UI issues and ensure high‑quality code through testing and reviews

Requirements
  • 3+ years of experience in front‑end development
  • Strong proficiency in React (preferred) or Angular/Vue
  • Expertise in responsive design, CSS frameworks (Tailwind, Bootstrap), and UI best practices
  • Experience integrating APIs and working with JSON/REST
  • Familiarity with Git, Agile workflows, and modern build tools (Webpack, Vite, etc.)
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to deliver clean, maintainable code

Preferred Qualifications
  • Experience with UI/UX tools (Figma, Adobe XD, Sketch)
  • Knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG)
  • Experience in startup or fast‑paced environments
  • Ability to contribute to design discussions and propose UI improvements

What We Offer
  • Opportunity to build high‑impact products in a highly scaling firm
  • Collaborative, innovative, and supportive team culture
  • Competitive compensation based on experience
  • Flexible remote work environment

For applications and inquiries, please email our Talent Team at : careers@apogeeglobalrms.com

jobs.title: Website Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

We believe that by equipping researchers with rapid insights and providers with tailored, on-demand data, we can help people lead better, healthier lives. At Digital Infuzion, we harness innovative healthcare solutions and cutting-edge bioinformatics to make meaningful impacts in patient care.

Our team thrives in a creative, open, and growth-oriented environment, guided by our core values:

Outcomes First: Focusing on what matters most and making timely, informed decisions.

Innovative: Embracing creativity and continuous improvement to drive novel solutions.

Radical Candor: Communicating openly and honestly, balancing direct feedback with genuine care.

Never Satisfied: Pursuing excellence and continuous growth beyond the status quo.

Resilient: Adapting and persevering through challenges, turning obstacles into opportunities.

If you're passionate about leveraging technology to improve healthcare and want to work in an environment that values innovation and collaboration, we may have just the opportunity for you.

Position Summary

The Website Developer designs, develops, tests, and maintains secure, scalable, and accessible web-based applications supporting clinical research and government programs. This role ensures modern, compliant, and user-friendly solutions that align with stakeholder requirements and federal standards. This is a work from home eligible position.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, test, and deploy responsive web applications.
  • Develop front-end solutions using HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, and modern frameworks.
  • Build and integrate back-end services and relational/NoSQL databases.
  • Ensure Section 508 compliance and accessibility standards.
  • Deploy and manage applications in AWS or Azure cloud environments.
  • Participate in Agile/Scrum development processes.
  • Conduct debugging, performance optimization, and security hardening.
  • Maintain technical documentation and collaborate with cross-functional teams.

Required Qualifications
  • Master’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field (8 years of experience may substitute).
  • Minimum 5 years of experience developing websites and web-based applications.
  • Experience integrating databases and cloud infrastructure.
  • Proficiency with JIRA, Confluence, and collaborative development tools.
  • Experience supporting Federal or State government web development projects.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Preferred Qualifications
  • Experience with clinical trial electronic data capture systems (CTDB, CTSS).

Digital Infuzion does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, retaliation, parental status, military service, or other non-merit factor per Federal laws.

We can provide reasonable accommodation to applicants with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application and hiring process, please contact Talent Acquisition at careers@digitalinfuzion.com. The decision on granting reasonable accommodation will be made on a case-by-case basis.

jobs.title: Sr. Front-End Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Job Summary


We are seeking a Sr. Front-End Web Developer to lead our Web Development team. The Sr. Front-End Web Developer will be responsible for the development and support of PFG’s web initiatives. This role will interact with key technology and business partners, vendors, and users to develop Front End eCommerce sites that support the objectives of the business. This role requires proven experience designing, implementing, and maintaining consumer facing eCommerce websites. This position is responsible for standing up sites using Vue js, React elements, set up tooling, CI/CD.


Major Responsibilities

  • Develops front-end portion of desktop and mobile web applications incorporating best practices and standards-based programming.
  • Designs overall user interface/user experience (UI/UX).
  • Implements strategies and adheres to best practices for Search Engine Optimization and vendor integration.
  • Works with application developers to integrate web GUI with back-end business applications in a 3-tier architecture.
  • Writes and develops code, conduct unit testing, communicate status, and escalate issues to team members and stakeholders, collaborating with project team and cross functional teams
  • Troubleshoots open issues and bug-fixes, enhances design to prevent re-occurrences of defects, ensures on-time delivery and hand-offs.


Key Competencies
  • Communicate well with the team and business partners.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders and agile team.
  • Ability to manage time and priorities.
  • Provide technical leadership to other developers in team.
  • Passionate and self-motivated


Other Internal/External Interactions
  • IT Personnel


Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor / Master’s in computer science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience
  • 5+ years of Software design and development experience using .NET Framework
  • Experience in the full software development life cycle
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Visual Studio, .NET Framework 4.5 +|Core
  • Strong command of web standards, responsive design, accessibility, and cross-browser compatibility
  • Experience working with CMS
  • Expert in HTML5, CSS3, SASS/LESS, jQuery and JavaScript
  • Extensive experience in developing Applications using ASP.NET Web Forms | ASP.NET MVC | ASP.NET Core
  • Experience in ASP.NET Web API | .NET Core API development and deployment
  • Experience in Client-Side Framework such as VueJS, ReactJS and Jekyll
  • Version Control Management: VSTS, GIT
  • Proficient knowledge and work experience in MSSQL | MySQL
  • Writing Unit Tests with NUnit | XUnit | MSTest
  • DevOps experience in CICD tools like Webpack, Azure, Octopus
  • Extensive working knowledge of Google Cloud Platform, Google Tag Management
  • Experience working with stakeholders to understand requirements


Physical Demands

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the major responsibilities.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit, stand or walk; use hands to finger, handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; stoop or bend; and talk or hear. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 50 lbs. Must have appropriate vision to see up close, and at a distance with ability to adjust vision and focus.


Position Type/Expected Hours of Work

This is a full-time position, days and hours of work are Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. This position might require long hours and weekend work.


Travel

N/A


Work Environment

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. The noise level in some work environments can be moderate.


Disclaimer

The above information on this description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted, as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, qualifications required of employees assigned to this job.


EEO Statement

Populus Financial Group is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state and local fair employment practices laws. In compliance with applicable law, Populus Financial Group does not discriminate against any applicant or employee on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender (including gender nonconformity and status as a transgender or transsexual individual), national origin or ancestry, ethnicity, age, physical or mental disability, citizenship, past, current, or prospective service in the uniformed services, genetic information, pregnancy status, or any other protected classification. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, training, promotions, discipline, compensation, benefits, and termination of employment.

jobs.title: Senior Frontend Developer - $200,000 + Equity - Austin, Remote
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Senior Frontend Developer - $200,000 + Equity - Austin, Remote first


We are working exclusively with an award-winning & fast growing start-up making waves with their innovative, first of it’s kind technology product suite tackling geospatial data and analytics like no one has done before. Their technology empowers enterprises and individuals to tackle real-world challenges using imagery and analytics - whether assessing bridge integrity, optimizing crop yields, tracking endangered species.


They are searching for a Senior Frontend Developer to join their software team. You will be responsible for design, delivery and maintenance of their system that enables frictionless user experience, integrate APIs, and maximizes usability while working within a cross functional team of backend developers, UI/UX and product.


Highlights & Requirements:


💰 Package: up to $200,000 + Benefits (Equity options, Remote first, Private medical & dental and much more

💻 Stack requirements: React, Next.JS, REST APIs, AWS, CI/CD pipelines

🚀 Industry/Type of business: Platform obtaining high-resolution Earth observation data and analytics

🏠 Remote first, yearly team meet-ups

🧩 Interview process: 2 stages, 5 day turn around.

😎 Reporting to: Head of Engineering

👏 Company size: 35 total (18 in engineering, cross functional)

jobs.title: Senior Front-End Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Front End Engineer (Web & Mobile)


We’re looking for a UX-minded Front-End Engineer to join our team and lead the development of cross-platform user experiences across web and mobile. You’ll work primarily in React Native to build high-quality, performant applications that run seamlessly on iOS, Android, and the web. You should be as comfortable pushing a new build to the App Store as you are shipping a new feature behind a feature flag on the web.


You’ve built and scaled front-end applications that serve millions of users, and you know how to design for performance, reliability, and user experience at scale. You thrive in cross-functional environments and bring a deep appreciation for design, quality, and usability.


We are looking to hire mid and senior level front end engineers!


You have drive, hunger, intellectual rigor. You are an owner.


This is a remote position. We are primarily looking for candidates based on the East Coast of the US or within Central European Time (CET ±2 hours) to ensure team overlap.


You will
  • Build and maintain cross-platform apps using React Native and related web technologies (e.g. React, Next.js)
  • Own feature development from design to deployment across iOS, Android, and web platforms
  • Leverage native iOS (Swift) code where necessary for performance or system integration
  • Collaborate with design, product, and backend engineering to bring features to life
  • Optimize performance, responsiveness, and accessibility across platforms
  • Help define and enforce front-end architecture, tooling, and code standards
  • Contribute to app store deployments and release management


You have
  • 5+ years of experience in front-end development, with significant time spent in React and React Native
  • A proven track record of building and maintaining front-end applications used by millions of users
  • Experience shipping production-quality mobile apps (iOS and/or Android)
  • Comfort working with native iOS (Swift or Objective-C) and integrating with React Native bridges
  • Strong understanding of front-end principles: component design, state management, mobile performance, etc.
  • Familiarity with app store processes (TestFlight, Apple App Store, Google Play)
  • A strong product sense and appreciation for good design and user experience
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills


Benefits and perks
  • Medical, Dental, Vision: We're excited to offer comprehensive and competitive benefits including Medical, Dental, Vision
  • Equal opportunity employer: We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.
  • 401k: We offer a 401(k) with several investment options for pre-tax retirement savings.


Culture
  • While we are remote-friendly, we are primarily hiring in the SF Bay Area and New York for non tech roles, and senior engineering across the SF Bay Area or Europe. Team members who join us should expect to come into the office at least a few days per week. If we hire you outside of an office location, you’ll be expected to be available during hours that overlap with the team.
  • Meld is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

jobs.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Job Title: Senior Frontend Engineer

Job Location: Fully Remote

Job Salary: $160 - $180k/yr DOE +Equity + full benefits!

Requirements: 6+ years of frontend engineering (React, TypeScript) + start up experience + AI skills/interests

Job Description:

We are pioneering the first wealth tech data orchestration platform, streamlining financial workflows, enhancing efficiency, and elevating client experiences. Committed to innovation and excellence, we're reshaping the landscape of finance.

We are seeking a talented front-end leaning engineer to spearhead the development of our early product experience. This individual will be instrumental in establishing our engineering culture, building end-to-end user-facing features, heavily influencing our UX Design system, and significantly contributing to the product roadmap and architectural choices.

Our Stack: React, TypeScript, MUI, React Query, Vite, Storybook, Playwright

Where you'll make an impact:
  • Lead development of major user flows (e.g., self-service onboarding, data reconciliation, digital account opening).
  • Define and enforce frontend architecture and standards for component abstraction, state management, performance, and accessibility.
  • Establish and scale the design system/UI toolkit collaboratively with Design and Product to ensure a polished and consistent experience.
  • Shape the technical vision and roadmap as an early team member, influencing key product and engineering decisions.
  • Elevate team efficiency by improving developer tooling, CI/CD, testing workflows, and mentoring new engineers as we grow.

Who you are:
  • A front end leaning engineer with 6+ years of experience delivering complex, high-performance and scalable user-facing systems
  • An expert in modern web development (preferably React), specializing in component architecture and refined UX.
  • High-ownership IC eager to contribute in an early-stage setting.
  • Action-oriented; proactively improves product and frontend architecture.
  • Customer-centric engineer with strong intuition for usability, accessibility, and product quality.


Bonus points if you have any experience in any of the following areas
  • FinTech
  • Early-stage startups (e.g., among the first three engineers)
  • Open-source software development and maintenance
  • Entrepreneurial spirit: founded a company or launched personal projects.
  • AI prompting, models, and tools, capable of mentoring others.

Benefits
  • Competitive salary and equity options
  • High-quality benefits package including 100% company-paid insurance premiums for family coverage (medical, vision, dental).
  • 401K, HSA & FSA plans
  • OneMedical, HealthAdvocate, TalkSpace, Teladoc memberships
  • Technology stipend
  • Unlimited PTO
  • Paid parental leave
  • Office space in San Francisco and New York for in-person working



Email Your Resume In Word To
Taylor.McAlister@CyberCoders.com
Looking forward to receiving your resume through our website and going over the position with you. Clicking apply is the best way to apply.
Please do NOT change the email subject line in any way. You must keep the JobID: linkedin : TM4-1978081 -- in the email subject line for your application to be considered.
Taylor McAlister - Sr. Executive Recruiter

For this position, you must be currently authorized to work in the United States without the need for sponsorship for a non-immigrant visa. This is a new role.

This job was first posted by CyberCoders on 02/17/2026 and applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled or closed.

This job was posted on 02/17/2026 and is open for 60 days

CyberCoders is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer

 All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, genetic information, registered domestic partner status, marital status, status as a crime victim, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Our hiring process includes AI screening for keywords and minimum qualifications. Recruiters review all results. CyberCoders will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and local law, including but not limited to the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and the California Fair Chance Act. jobs.title: Sr. Frontend Developer (React Native)
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

We’re partnering with the team behind one of the most widely used fantasy sports and gaming platforms who serve millions of passionate users & is backed by top-tier investors. Their product is mobile-first, socially driven, & scaling fast across new game formats and user communities.


As a Senior Frontend Developer, you’ll take full ownership of feature development across mobile platforms, using a modern stack that includes TypeScript, React Query, React Navigation, and Reanimated.


What You’ll Do
  • Design, build, and ship features across mobile platforms using React Native and TypeScript
  • Collaborate directly with designers and fellow engineers to shape features based on user behavior and product goals
  • Create and maintain a well-structured, reusable component system to ensure design consistency and efficiency across the app
  • Own app logic, UI interactions, and state management using hooks and modern React patterns
  • Integrate with backend services via REST and GraphQL
  • Troubleshoot performance, caching, and socket issues
  • Contribute to overall codebase quality, tooling, and long-term maintainability


What They’re Looking For
  • 6+ years of frontend or mobile development experience
  • 3+ years working with React Native in production
  • Experienced in TypeScript, hooks, component architecture, and debugging performance issues
  • Previously worked on a consumer product with millions of users
  • Enjoy fast-paced teams, autonomy, and shaping product decisions alongside design
  • Bonus: Started as a native iOS or Android engineer before moving into RN

jobs.title: Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Position: Web Developer

Compensation: $45-$57/hr, depending on experience

Location: Remote

Inceed has partnered with a great company to help find a skilled Web Developer to join their team!

This is a fully remote opportunity for a passionate Web Developer eager to grow and innovate. The role is open for a dynamic individual who loves working with cutting-edge technology and is ready to make a difference.

Key Responsibilities & Duties:
  • Develop complex web applications using Microsoft MVC pattern
  • Work with Asp.Net MVC and .Net Core MVC
  • Implement data access using Microsoft Entity Framework
  • Engage in business logic programming and web-tier components
  • Craft dynamic UI with JavaScript and jQuery
  • Handle AJAX communications between JavaScript and MVC/Web API Controllers
  • Ensure code quality with clear documentation and consistent patterns
  • Participate in daily Scrum stand-ups

Required Qualifications & Experience:
  • Proficiency in C# and JavaScript
  • Experience with Microsoft Entity Framework
  • Strong skills in Razor markup and CSS styling
  • Familiarity with Bootstrap 4 and 5
  • Comfortable with direct JavaScript programming
  • Excellent communication skills

Nice to Have Skills & Experience:
  • SQL and Microsoft SQL Server knowledge
  • Experience with Telerik and Syncfusion libraries

Perks & Benefits:
  • 3 different medical health insurance plans, dental, and vision insurance
  • Voluntary and Long-term disability insurance
  • Paid time off, 401k, and holiday pay
  • Weekly direct deposit or pay card deposit

Other Information:
  • This position requires a background check
  • Flexible schedule with availability during some PST time

If you are interested in learning more about the Web Developer opportunity, please submit your resume for consideration. Our client is unable to provide sponsorship at this time.

We are Inceed, a staffing direct placement firm who believes in the possibility of something better. Our mission is simple: We’re here to help every person, whether client, candidate, or employee, find and secure what’s better for them.

Inceed is an equal opportunity employer. Inceed prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type and affords equal employment opportunities to employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Front End
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Tebra only initiates contact with candidates via email from an official Tebra email address (@tebra.com, @patientpop.com, or @kareo.com) or through our applicant tracking system, Greenhouse. We will only ask you to provide sensitive personal information through our official application portal — not via social media or text message. We do not conduct interviews via instant messaging.

About the Role

As a Staff Software Engineer, Front End, you will take on a critical technical leadership role, tackling meaningful, real-world challenges for healthcare providers. Your primary mission is to lead the architectural direction for our most ambitious, large-scale UI initiatives. We are counting on your expertise to guide this technical transformation, championing a customer-first mindset while making the thoughtful, pragmatic decisions that ensure long-term technical health. Concurrently, you will be a key pioneer in integrating AI into our products, shaping and building the new, intelligent user experiences that will define our next chapter.

Your Area of Focus
  • Own a product end-to-end from defining the problem we should solve to meet our OKRs to designing, building, shipping, and operating the solution.
  • Act as a key technical partner for Product Management, co-owning the roadmap, identifying key risks, and surfacing new engineering-led opportunities.
  • Rapidly form hypotheses based on data and iterate quickly to find tactics that deliver the biggest impact. Rinse and repeat.
  • Focus on what’s right, not who. Solicit feedback, learn, teach, and give candid feedback to grow and improve together. Continuously.
  • Architect & code frameworks that can be leveraged by the team.
  • Invest in technical foundations to accelerate our shipping rhythm. Ship products worthy of recognition.
  • Champion the integration of AI tools (like coding assistants) into our daily development lifecycle to boost team velocity, improve code quality, and streamline testing.
Your Professional Qualifications
  • 8+ years of software development experience including building production-grade web applications using React, Vue, Angular, or Material UI.
  • Mastery in utilizing REST/GraphQL APIs efficiently, influencing the shape of APIs where necessary.
  • In-depth knowledge of JavaScript/TypeScript, CSS, NodeJS, and the tooling ecosystem (NPM, Yarn, Babel, Jest, Cypress) to the extent you can describe its limitations and tradeoffs.
  • Deep understanding of the tradeoffs between static generation, server-side rendering, and client-side rendering.
  • Exceptional level of engineering fundamentals including a strong grasp of data structures, caching strategies, and asynchronous programming (promises, futures, callbacks).
  • Understanding and experience with monorepo environments and microfrontend architecture.
  • Led and shipped large-scale projects from conception to production including post-launch support involving multiple teams.
  • Have development experience working with LLMs, AI-driven UX, or agentic workflows.
  • Desire to blaze the trail on less-glorious engineering tasks that are important to the business.
  • Constructively dissatisfied with the status quo.
  • Comfortable working on problems that do not have well defined requirements.
  • Experience building and mentoring software engineering teams.
  • Can drive difficult engineering discussions with the team and present a menu of options to management.
  • Able to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ability to partner with product management to develop requirements.
  • Well versed at recognizing project risks and opportunities.
  • Able to make build vs. buy software decisions.
  • Able to look beyond software best practices to make tough technical decisions that are tailored to business needs.
  • Familiarity with query and analytical tools such as SQL, Google Analytics and FullStory.

(For Recruiter use only)

In compliance with California's pay transparency laws, the compensation range for this position will be provided and may include an hourly rate, annual salary, or On-Target Earnings (OTE), depending on the nature of the role. The specific compensation structure and detailed range will be discussed with qualified candidates during the initial talent screen.

Remote Pay Range: $169,500 USD - $189,750 USD

 About Tebra

Kareo and PatientPop have joined forces to become Tebra, the digital backbone for practice well-being. While our teams are still supporting both products, our new hires and current employees are now united as Team Tebra.

Tebra aims to unlock better healthcare by helping independent practices bring modernized care to patients everywhere. Well over 100,000 providers trust Tebra to elevate their patient experience, and help them grow their practice. At Tebra, we’re building the future of well-being together. That shared vision for tomorrow begins with compassion and humanity today.

Our ValuesStart with the Customer

We get to know our customers - and their patients - and look at the world through their lens.

Keep It Simple

Healthcare is too complex. We aim to simplify it for everyone.

Stay Entrepreneurial

We reject the status quo and solve problems with creativity, perseverance, and a bias to action.

Better Together

We are diverse, humble, and collaborative. We put the team first and win together.

Celebrate Success

Life is short and joy is underrated. We take time to have fun and celebrate success.

Perks & Benefits

United States: In addition to our healthcare benefits, we also offer amazing perks! Need work from home basics? We offer a discount through Dell! We also offer a number of resources to help you keep your mind and body healthy. Check out Gympass for a great workout, or TelusEmployee Assistance Program to find mental health resources, along with other resources for everyday occurrences.

Costa Rica: To assist with all of life’s needs, Tebra also offers a wellness and childcare subsidy and a University/Education discount! We also offer a number of resources to help you keep your mind and body healthy. Check out Gympass for access to health and fitness apps, or Telus Employee Assistance Program to find mental health resources, along with other resources for everyday occurrences.

Compliance & Privacy Disclosures

NOTE: Tebra is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

California residents who apply or are recruited for a job with us: please carefully review our California-specific Privacy Notice under the California Consumer Protection Act here: https://www.tebra.com/privacy-policy/california-supplemental-notice/

If you would like to report a fraudulent Tebra job posting, please contact us at talentacquisition@tebra.com and consider reporting your experience to the FBI's Internet Crime

About the Role

As a Staff Software Engineer, Front End, you will take on a critical technical leadership role, tackling meaningful, real-world challenges for healthcare providers. Your primary mission is to lead the architectural direction for our most ambitious, large-scale UI initiatives. We are counting on your expertise to guide this technical transformation, championing a customer-first mindset while making the thoughtful, pragmatic decisions that ensure long-term technical health. Concurrently, you will be a key pioneer in integrating AI into our products, shaping and building the new, intelligent user experiences that will define our next chapter.

Your Area of Focus
  • Own a product end-to-end from defining the problem we should solve to meet our OKRs to designing, building, shipping, and operating the solution.
  • Act as a key technical partner for Product Management, co-owning the roadmap, identifying key risks, and surfacing new engineering-led opportunities.
  • Rapidly form hypotheses based on data and iterate quickly to find tactics that deliver the biggest impact. Rinse and repeat.
  • Focus on what’s right, not who. Solicit feedback, learn, teach, and give candid feedback to grow and improve together. Continuously.
  • Architect & code frameworks that can be leveraged by the team.
  • Invest in technical foundations to accelerate our shipping rhythm. Ship products worthy of recognition.
  • Champion the integration of AI tools (like coding assistants) into our daily development lifecycle to boost team velocity, improve code quality, and streamline testing.
Your Professional Qualifications
  • 8+ years of software development experience including building production-grade web applications using React, Vue, Angular, or Material UI.
  • Mastery in utilizing REST/GraphQL APIs efficiently, influencing the shape of APIs where necessary.
  • In-depth knowledge of JavaScript/TypeScript, CSS, NodeJS, and the tooling ecosystem (NPM, Yarn, Babel, Jest, Cypress) to the extent you can describe its limitations and tradeoffs.
  • Deep understanding of the tradeoffs between static generation, server-side rendering, and client-side rendering.
  • Exceptional level of engineering fundamentals including a strong grasp of data structures, caching strategies, and asynchronous programming (promises, futures, callbacks).
  • Understanding and experience with monorepo environments and microfrontend architecture.
  • Led and shipped large-scale projects from conception to production including post-launch support involving multiple teams.
  • Have development experience working with LLMs, AI-driven UX, or agentic workflows.
  • Desire to blaze the trail on less-glorious engineering tasks that are important to the business.
  • Constructively dissatisfied with the status quo.
  • Comfortable working on problems that do not have well defined requirements.
  • Experience building and mentoring software engineering teams.
  • Can drive difficult engineering discussions with the team and present a menu of options to management.
  • Able to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ability to partner with product management to develop requirements.
  • Well versed at recognizing project risks and opportunities.
  • Able to make build vs. buy software decisions.
  • Able to look beyond software best practices to make tough technical decisions that are tailored to business needs.
  • Familiarity with query and analytical tools such as SQL, Google Analytics and FullStory.

(For Recruiter use only)

In compliance with California's pay transparency laws, the compensation range for this position will be provided and may include an hourly rate, annual salary, or On-Target Earnings (OTE), depending on the nature of the role. The specific compensation structure and detailed range will be discussed with qualified candidates during the initial talent screen.

Remote Pay Range: $169,500 USD - $189,750 USD

 About Tebra

Kareo and PatientPop have joined forces to become Tebra, the digital backbone for practice well-being. While our teams are still supporting both products, our new hires and current employees are now united as Team Tebra.

Tebra aims to unlock better healthcare by helping independent practices bring modernized care to patients everywhere. Well over 100,000 providers trust Tebra to elevate their patient experience, and help them grow their practice. At Tebra, we’re building the future of well-being together. That shared vision for tomorrow begins with compassion and humanity today.

Our ValuesStart with the Customer

We get to know our customers - and their patients - and look at the world through their lens.

Keep It Simple

Healthcare is too complex. We aim to simplify it for everyone.

Stay Entrepreneurial

We reject the status quo and solve problems with creativity, perseverance, and a bias to action.

Better Together

We are diverse, humble, and collaborative. We put the team first and win together.

Celebrate Success

Life is short and joy is underrated. We take time to have fun and celebrate success.

Perks & Benefits

United States: In addition to our healthcare benefits, we also offer amazing perks! Need work from home basics? We offer a discount through Dell! We also offer a number of resources to help you keep your mind and body healthy. Check out Gympass for a great workout, or TelusEmployee Assistance Program to find mental health resources, along with other resources for everyday occurrences.

Costa Rica: To assist with all of life’s needs, Tebra also offers a wellness and childcare subsidy and a University/Education discount! We also offer a number of resources to help you keep your mind and body healthy. Check out Gympass for access to health and fitness apps, or Telus Employee Assistance Program to find mental health resources, along with other resources for everyday occurrences.

Compliance & Privacy Disclosures

NOTE: Tebra is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

California residents who apply or are recruited for a job with us: please carefully review our California-specific Privacy Notice under the California Consumer Protection Act here: https://www.tebra.com/privacy-policy/california-supplemental-notice/

If you would like to report a fraudulent Tebra job posting, please contact us at talentacquisition@tebra.com and consider reporting your experience to the FBI's Internet Crime

jobs.title: Lead Front-End Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Summary

We are seeking a skilled and experienced Frontend Development Lead to design and build intuitive, high-performance user interfaces for a cutting-edge financial platform. This role involves leading the UI/UX discipline from inception, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and delivering scalable, secure web applications that simplify complex financial workflows for enterprise and public-sector clients. The position offers a blend of leadership and hands-on development within a fast-growing, innovative environment.


Requirements
  • Minimum of 5 years of front-end development experience building modern web applications
  • Strong proficiency in JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML5, and CSS3
  • Expertise in React (preferred)
  • Experience integrating with .NET / C# APIs in a Microsoft-based environment
  • Familiarity with Azure DevOps for source control, CI/CD, and deployment pipelines
  • Knowledge of RESTful APIs, JSON, OAuth2, JWT, and secure integration patterns
  • Understanding of cross-browser compatibility, WCAG accessibility standards, and responsive design principles
  • Experience with state management libraries (Redux, Zustand, MobX, or similar)
  • Exposure to UI testing tools (Playwright, Cypress, Jest) and agile methodologies
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (preferred)


Responsibilities
  • Build responsive, high-performance, accessible web applications using modern JavaScript frameworks, primarily React
  • Develop and maintain reusable UI components and style libraries to ensure consistency across modules and white-label variations
  • Collaborate with backend developers to consume RESTful APIs, implement authentication, and troubleshoot API-related issues
  • Lead the translation of wireframes, prototypes, and design systems into production-quality code while contributing to the company’s visual identity and UX standards
  • Optimize application performance and load times for enterprise-grade deployments
  • Participate in sprint planning, peer code reviews, and contribute to CI/CD workflows within Azure or GitHub actions
  • Establish and document front-end standards and best practices for the growing development team
  • Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure technical alignment with product goals, scalability, usability, and security standards


Benefits
  • Join a rapidly growing, greenfield development environment with a new, dynamic team
  • Opportunity to lead and establish UI/UX practices from the ground up
  • Flexible, fully remote position offering work-life balance and growth in a cutting-edge fintech environment
