jobs.title: Staff Frontend Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Company Overview

Fanfix is a premier content platform dedicated to empowering creators and fostering meaningful connections with their fans. We provide a safe, brand-friendly environment where creators can pursue their passions and monetize their work. With a global community of over 7 million fans, our platform enables engagement through features like direct messaging, memberships, tipping, and exclusive content. We’re committed to continuous innovation, building high-performance web applications that elevate the creator and fan experience.


Role Overview

As a Staff Frontend Engineer at Fanfix, you will play a key role in designing and developing complex, scalable web applications that drive meaningful interactions between creators and their fans. You’ll be responsible for contributing to frontend development efforts, collaborating closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver seamless and high-performing features. With a strong focus on React, TypeScript, and testing frameworks like Jest, you’ll ensure the quality, maintainability, and scalability of the application.

In addition to hands-on development, you’ll contribute to technical discussions, provide guidance to junior engineers, and support architectural decisions that shape the future of our platform. This role requires a proactive problem solver who thrives in a fast-paced startup environment and is passionate about continuously improving the tech stack while driving innovative solutions. A key aspect of the role is taking ownership of critical frontend features, ensuring they align with business goals and meet performance and scalability standards. This role will report to our Head of Engineering.

If you’re an expert in frontend technologies, comfortable in a fast-paced work environment, and possess strong technical skills, we’d love for you to join us in revolutionizing the way creators and fans connect.


*This is a remote position.


Requirements
  • Next.js & React: Expert-level experience building complex, scalable applications with Next.js + React, including App Router, Server Components, SSR/SSG patterns, and Next.js optimization techniques.
  • TypeScript: Proficiency in TypeScript 5+ for type-safe development, ensuring cleaner, more maintainable code across a large codebase.
  • Vitest & Testing Library: Hands-on experience with Vitest and Testing Library for unit and integration testing, ensuring quality and stability of the application.
  • GraphQL & Apollo Client: Strong experience with Apollo Client for managing application data, including type-safe queries/mutations, cache management, and working with GraphQL Code Generator for TypeScript integration.
  • Tailwind CSS: Proficiency with Tailwind CSS and utility-first styling patterns for rapid UI development. Experience with CSS-in-JS libraries (Styled Components or Emotion) is a plus.
  • State Management: Experience with modern state management solutions including Zustand and Redux Toolkit, with the ability to determine the appropriate solution based on complexity and use case.
  • Monorepo Architecture: Experience working in monorepo codebases using pnpm workspaces and Turbo (or similar tools like Nx/Lerna) for build orchestration and code sharing.
  • Node.js & Server-Side Development: Understanding of Node.js for working with Next.js server-side features including API routes, server components, server actions, and middleware, enabling effective collaboration with backend teams.
  • Attention to Detail: Demonstrates precision and thoroughness in code development, testing, and documentation, especially when working with complex state management and data flows.


Preferred Qualifications
  • Component Libraries: Familiarity with multiple UI libraries (Material-UI, Ant Design, HeroUI, Radix UI) and headless component patterns, with the ability to work across different design systems.
  • Authentication: Experience with NextAuth or similar authentication solutions for Next.js applications, including OAuth flows and session management.
  • Performance Optimization: Proven track record of optimizing frontend performance using Web Vitals, image optimization, code splitting, and Next.js-specific performance patterns.
  • Strong Communication Skills: Proven experience in effectively communicating technical concepts and collaborating with team members across functions.
  • Proactive Problem Solver: Ability to identify potential problems early and propose effective solutions to improve performance, scalability, and code quality.
  • Adaptable: Comfortable in a fast-paced startup environment, adapting to shifting priorities and new challenges while maintaining code quality standards.


Responsibilities
  • Application Development: Design, develop, and maintain complex application features and systems, ensuring they meet performance, scalability, and quality standards.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate with cross-functional teams (product, design, backend) to design and implement solutions that meet both business needs and technical constraints.
  • Mentorship Support: Support and provide technical guidance to junior engineers as needed.
  • Performance and Scalability: Contribute to performance improvements across the application, ensuring it can scale effectively as user demands grow.
  • Feature Ownership: Take ownership of critical frontend features or modules, ensuring their timely and high-quality delivery.
  • Architectural Contribution: Participate in architectural discussions and contribute to strategic decisions that help shape the future of the application’s technical roadmap.


Benefits

  • Compensation: Base salary ($160K - $200K) + Annual Bonus
  • 100% Employer sponsored medical, dental and vision insurance, including all dependents!
  • 401k
  • Generous PTO / paid holidays
  • Parental leave
  • Fun working environment at a high growth tech startup!

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Frontend
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

*This role supports US-based remote work, or Palo Alto, CA based hybrid in-office.


We’re looking for Software Engineers to join our team. As part of a cross-functional scrum team you will design and develop new products and features to help clients grow their wealth. In addition to building client facing features leveraging our in-house Design System components, you also work on improving our infrastructure and developer productivity and can have significant impact afforded by our cross-platform collaboration methods.



As a core member of the Frontend team, you will:
  • Collaborate with backend and mobile engineers, designers, and product managers to build new features and enhance existing features
  • Join other web engineers to triage and resolve operational issues, ensuring that mission-critical web systems and services operate smoothly across the web platform
  • Help with web platform infrastructure projects to improve the health and performance of our systems and increase product delivery velocity
  • Participate in recruiting activities such as interviewing
  • Provide mentorship to other engineers on the team


This role could be a fit if you have:
  • 2-5 years in a web-focused engineering role
  • A deep understanding of JavaScript, including standard ES5+, native DOM APIs, and ideally at least one modern front-end framework (React, Vue, Angular, etc)
  • Experience with Ruby on Rails or similar server-side frameworks (such as Express or Django) is a plus
  • Experience working with design to build custom pages and components, with a focus on page speed performance and writing custom CSS to create pages that are responsive across a variety of screen sizes
  • Real passion for working on projects that have a measurable business impact and like working in a fast-paced cross-functional environment
  • Experience with test-driven development, continuous integration, and automated testing
  • You have either a BS or MS in computer science or related field.


Once you join, Wealthfront will:
  • Pair you with a mentor who will guide you through our structured onboarding program
  • Encourage you to work on projects that match your professional goals
  • Support your professional development by providing feedback during weekly 1:1s and our bi-annual reviews
  • Provide autonomy so you can be a happy and successful member of our team



Estimated annual salary range: $140,000 - $185,000 plus equity and a discretionary bonus.


Benefits include medical, vision, dental, 401K plan, generous time off, parental leave, wellness reimbursements, professional development, employee investing discount, and more!


About Wealthfront


Here at Wealthfront, our mission is to create a financial system that favors people, not institutions. We do this by leveraging technology to build powerful, low-cost, and easy-to-use financial products that help modern investors grow and manage their money.


We started with the ambition to transform the investment advisory business. By automating strategies typically reserved for the wealthy, we unlocked access to high quality investment advice for a digitally-native generation that was underserved by traditional institutions. Since then, we've expanded to a full suite of products designed to help our clients turn their savings into long-term wealth, including:


• A Cash Account that, through our partner banks, offers one of the highest annual percentage yields on uninvested cash in the industry, while providing instant and secure access to your money with no account fees and a full suite of checking features.


• A zero-commission Stock Investing Account with 50+ handpicked collections that help DIY investors discover new companies and make smarter investing decisions.


• Multiple automated investing portfolios designed to unlock tax savings through sophisticated strategies like fixed income, tax-loss harvesting, and direct indexing—which we offer at industry-leading low costs and accessible minimums.


Our award-winning products have attracted over 1 million clients who trust us with more than $90 billion of their hard earned savings—and we're far from done. If you’re inspired to help us reshape the financial industry as we create our next chapter, let’s talk!


For more information please visit www.wealthfront.com.We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.



Please review our candidate privacy notice.



Disclosures: All investing involves risk, including the possible loss of principal. Tax-Loss Harvesting benefits vary depending on the client's entire tax and investment profile. Wealthfront doesn’t provide tax advice. The Cash Account is offered by Wealthfront Brokerage LLC (“Wealthfront Brokerage”), Member of FINRA/SIPC. Wealthfront Brokerage is not a bank. We convey funds to partner banks who accept and maintain deposits, provide the variable interest rate, and provide access to FDIC pass-through insurance. Investment management and advisory services–which are not FDIC insured–are provided by Wealthfront Advisers LLC (“Wealthfront Advisers”), an SEC-registered investment adviser. The checking features offered in the Wealthfront Cash Account are provided by Green Dot Bank, Member FDIC. Fees and Eligibility requirements may apply to certain checking features, please see the Deposit Account Agreement for details.



By “award-winning products”, please refer to www.wealthfront.com/reviews for more information. Wealthfront Corporation oversees Total Client Assets and Trusted Clients through Wealthfront Advisers and Wealthfront Brokerage. Wealthfront Advisers and Wealthfront Brokerage are wholly owned subsidiaries of Wealthfront Corporation.

jobs.title: Front End Design Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Responsibilities

Kforce has a client in Ramsey, NJ that is seeking a Front End Design Developer. Summary: This design-focused resource will help define what the business and users are trying to answer before development begins. They will work upfront on mockups and iterative designs (e.g. questions to the business and user interfacing side: If the data were displayed this way, would you be able to find the answers you need?- and then go find a solution if not). Currently, this upfront design work isn't happening, which is leading to heavy, unnecessary development cycles. The Developer will be coming up with a plan to build out this organization and help evaluate the org holistically, including establishing a high-level design approach and operating model. Ideal candidate: strong design background with some front-end development experience. They should be able to get up to speed on Looker components quickly so they can bridge mockups with what Looker can deliver. Responsibilities:

  • Experience Design: Interview users and understand their needs as it fits into the larger picture
  • Visual Design: Mock-up designs (leveraging Looker components) to facilitate conversations and get feedback before moving forward with development
  • Component Design/Develop: Design and develop new components that can be added to the Looker component library that meet needs for data visualization that we don't currently have
  • Custom FE Dev: Leverage the Looker Extension Framework - requires familiarity with JavaScript and specifically with the React framework

Requirements
  • To be considered for this position, candidates must have experience in a similar role, or they must possess significant knowledge, experience, and abilities to successfully perform the responsibilities listed
  • Relevant education and/or training will be considered a plus
  • Looker experience is nice to have

jobs.title: Front End Design Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Responsibilities

Kforce has a client in Ramsey, NJ that is seeking a Front End Design Developer. Summary: This design-focused resource will help define what the business and users are trying to answer before development begins. They will work upfront on mockups and iterative designs (e.g. questions to the business and user interfacing side: If the data were displayed this way, would you be able to find the answers you need?- and then go find a solution if not). Currently, this upfront design work isn't happening, which is leading to heavy, unnecessary development cycles. The Developer will be coming up with a plan to build out this organization and help evaluate the org holistically, including establishing a high-level design approach and operating model. Ideal candidate: strong design background with some front-end development experience. They should be able to get up to speed on Looker components quickly so they can bridge mockups with what Looker can deliver. Responsibilities:

  • Experience Design: Interview users and understand their needs as it fits into the larger picture
  • Visual Design: Mock-up designs (leveraging Looker components) to facilitate conversations and get feedback before moving forward with development
  • Component Design/Develop: Design and develop new components that can be added to the Looker component library that meet needs for data visualization that we don't currently have
  • Custom FE Dev: Leverage the Looker Extension Framework - requires familiarity with JavaScript and specifically with the React framework

Requirements
  • To be considered for this position, candidates must have experience in a similar role, or they must possess significant knowledge, experience, and abilities to successfully perform the responsibilities listed

jobs.title: Full Stack TypeScript Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

3RD PARTY CANDIDATES WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED!!

Candidates must be local to Atlanta, GA


We’re looking for a Senior Full-Stack TypeScript Developer who can autonomously own features across web and mobile platforms with minimal supervision. In this role, you’ll work with TypeScript across the full stack — building user-facing experiences with React (web) and React Native (mobile) and developing backend APIs with Node.js (NestJS experience preferred), all within modern, containerized environments. You’ll take full ownership of features end-to-end, make independent technical decisions, and mentor junior team members while writing clean, maintainable code that sets the standard for the team.


While the role is remote, candidates must be in the metro Atlanta area.


AI-Forward Software Delivery

SOLTECH is an early adopter of AI-powered software delivery. Our engineers work in an AI-forward environment where modern AI development tools are used to accelerate coding, improve design decisions, and enhance engineering productivity.

We actively help clients build AI-enabled applications, enterprise AI agents, and AI-centric SaaS platforms across industries. This means our engineers are not only using AI to build software faster, but also designing and delivering software that incorporates AI as a core capability.

Engineers who thrive at SOLTECH are curious, innovative, and eager to explore how AI can transform the way software is designed, built, and delivered.


Core Requirements

Essential Skills
  • 5–7+ years of professional development experience in production environments
  • Strong TypeScript proficiency with a type-first design mindset across the full stack
  • Solid experience with React (web) and React Native (mobile) development
  • Backend API development experience with Node.js (NestJS experience preferred)
  • Strong proficiency in relational databases, including schema design, query optimization, and performance tuning
  • Ability to independently deliver complete features across the full stack with minimal guidance
  • Proficiency with AI tools and models in software engineering, including:Using AI-assisted coding tools to accelerate development workflows
  • Critical thinking about when to leverage AI versus traditional problem-solving approaches
  • Mentoring junior developers on effective and responsible AI tool usage
  • Comfortable working with multiple developer tools and command-line interfaces, including:Linux environments
  • Docker and containerization
  • Standard command-line operations


Professional and Collaboration Skills
  • Autonomous decision-maker who can navigate architectural trade-offs independently
  • Self-starter with a proven track record of owning features from conception to production
  • Problem-solver who writes clean, maintainable code and establishes coding standards
  • Team player who can mentor junior developers and communicate effectively about technical decisions and blockers
  • Comfortable working in lean teams with high autonomy and ownership

Bonus Skills
  • Multi-language proficiency: Demonstrated ability to develop in languages beyond TypeScript
  • Mobile development: React Native or similar mobile development experience
  • Cloud platforms: AWS or Azure experience
  • DevOps and infrastructure: Exposure to tools like Terraform, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, or CI/CD pipelines
  • Deno: Experience with Deno as a runtime environment

jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Hiring: Founding Backend Software Engineer (Remote)


We’re a fast-growing startup building an AI-powered recruiting platform that unifies the hiring lifecycle end to end. We’re looking for a Founding Software Engineer to help build and scale our core backend systems.


What you’ll do:
  • Build scalable backend services in TypeScript (NestJS preferred)
  • Design REST & GraphQL APIs
  • Work with PostgreSQL, Redis, and modern cloud infrastructure
  • Collaborate with AI, frontend, and product teams
  • Own architecture, performance, and reliability


What we’re looking for:
  • 4+ years backend engineering experience
  • Strong Node.js / TypeScript skills
  • Experience with APIs, databases, Docker, and CI/CD
  • Startup mindset and high ownership


Why join:
  • Early engineer impact + meaningful equity
  • Competitive salary ($100K–$200K) + bonuses
  • Remote-first, flexible, high-trust culture
  • Clear growth path toward Engineering Lead


Interested? Apply or DM to learn more.

jobs.title: REMOTE Sr Frontend Engineer - B2B SaaS Startup - React, Typescri
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Founded in 2022 and backed by investors behind SpaceX, Anduril, Neuralink, Palantir, and Reddit, we're a B2B SaaS Startup tackling the $80T financial advisory market that still relies on outdated processes. We're building the first wealth tech data orchestration platform to streamline workflows, boost efficiency, sync data, and transform the client experience.

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Staff Frontend Engineer to join our dynamic team in a remote capacity. The ideal candidate will play a critical role in developing innovative frontend solutions for our B2B SaaS platform, helping to improve user experience and interface design. This position offers an opportunity to work in a fast-paced startup environment where creativity and technical expertise are valued.

Key Responsibilities
  • Design and implement user-friendly and responsive web applications using React and TypeScript.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and ship new features.
  • Optimize applications for maximum speed and scalability.
  • Ensure the technical feasibility of UI/UX designs and implement them effectively.
  • Maintain code quality, organization, and automation through version control (GIT).
  • Conduct code reviews and provide mentorship to junior developers.
  • Work in an Agile development environment, participating in sprint planning and retrospectives.
  • Develop and maintain RESTful APIs and ensure cross-browser compatibility.

Qualifications
  • BSCS or related degree
  • Proven experience in frontend development with expertise in TypeScript and React.
  • Experience in building and scaling SaaS applications in a startup environment.
  • Strong understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
  • Familiarity with User Interface (UI) Design and Responsive Design principles.
  • Experience with Agile methodologies and version control systems (GIT).
  • Knowledge of testing frameworks and best practices for frontend applications.
  • Experience with modern tools and libraries such as MUI, React Query, Vite, Storybook, and Playwright is a plus.

Benefits
  • 100% Remote work
  • Competitive salary and equity options
  • High quality benefits package including 100% company paid insurance premiums for family coverage
  • OneMedical, HealthAdvocate, TalkSpace, Teladoc memberships
  • Technology stipend
  • Unlimited PTO
  • Paid parental leave
  • Beautiful office spaces in San Francisco and New York

jobs.title: Full Stack Developer (Remote)
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

About the Role

We are seeking a Full Stack Developer to join our team in building secure, scalable, and high-performance enterprise applications.


In this role, you will design, develop, and maintain modern full-stack solutions using .NET, Angular, and cloud-based data platforms, while collaborating with cross-functional teams including architects, product owners, and data engineers.


You will contribute to end-to-end software delivery, working on both front-end user interfaces and back-end services, while assisting with modernizing our digital platforms, automate workflows, and improve data integration.


Key Responsibilities

Application Development:
  • Design, develop, and maintain full-stack applications using .NET, C#, and Angular
  • Build scalable RESTful APIs and backend services using modern .NET frameworks
  • Develop responsive and intuitive front-end interfaces using Angular
  • Implement reusable UI components and maintain high standards for usability and performance


Workflow & Business Process Automation:
  • Design and implement workflow solutions using K2
  • Develop business process automation and integrate workflows with enterprise systems
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business requirements into technical solutions


Data Integration & Data Engineering:
  • Develop and maintain SQL-based data solutions
  • Build data pipelines and integrations using Azure Data Factory (ADF)
  • Ensure data integrity, security, and performance within enterprise data environments


Consulting & Collaboration:
  • Work closely with client stakeholders, architects, and product teams to deliver technical solutions
  • Participate in Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, stand-ups, and retrospectives
  • Provide technical input on architecture, performance optimization, and best practices


Quality & Best Practices:
  • Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code
  • Conduct code reviews and contribute to continuous improvement of development standards
  • Ensure compliance with security, regulatory, and financial industry standards


Required Qualifications:
  • Full-stack development experience
  • Strong experience with:
  • .NET Framework / .NET Core
  • C#
  • Angular
  • SQL / relational databases
  • Experience with K2 workflow development
  • Experience building front-end (FE) and back-end enterprise applications
  • Experience developing REST APIs and microservices
  • Experience with Azure Data Factory (ADF) or similar data integration tools
  • Strong understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) and Agile methodologies


Preferred Qualifications:
  • Experience with Azure cloud services
  • Knowledge of data integration, ETL pipelines, and enterprise data architecture
  • Familiarity with DevOps practices and CI/CD pipelines
  • Experience supporting digital transformation initiatives in enterprise environments

jobs.title: Front-End Engineer, Subscriptions & Payments
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

At Webflow, we’re building the world’s leading AI-native Digital Experience Platform, and we’re doing it as a remote-first company built on trust, transparency, and a whole lot of creativity. This work takes grit, because we move fast, without ever sacrificing craft or quality. Our mission is to bring development superpowers to everyone. From entrepreneurs launching their first idea to global enterprises scaling their digital presence, we empower teams to design, launch, and optimize for the web without barriers. We believe the future of the web, and work, is more open, more creative, and more equitable. And we’re here to build it together.

We're looking for a Front-End Engineer to join the Subscription & Payments team to define how millions of customers purchase and manage subscriptions. You will design and ship plan selection, checkout, and billing management UIs that are secure, performant, and accessible. Working with product, design, and cross-functional teams, you will influence technical direction, build services to speed up monetization launches, and improve conversion and retention.

About The Role

  • Location: Remote-first (United States; BC & ON, Canada)
  • Full-time
  • Permanent
  • Exempt
  • The cash compensation for this role is tailored to align with the cost of labor in different geographic markets. We've structured the base pay ranges for this role into zones for our geographic markets, and the specific base pay within the range will be determined by the candidate’s geographic location, job-related experience, knowledge, qualifications, and skills.
    • United States (all figures cited below are in USD and pertain to workers in the United States)
      • Zone A: $134,000 - $197,000
      • Zone B: $126,000 - $185,000
      • Zone C: $118,000 - $173,000
    • Canada (figures cited below are in CAD and pertain to workers in ON & BC, Canada)
      • $152,000 - $223,000
This role is also eligible to participate in Webflow's company-wide bonus program. Target amounts are a percentage of base salary and vary by career level. Payouts are based on company performance against established financial and operational goals.

Please visit our Careers page for more information on which locations are included in each of our geographic pay zones. However, please confirm the zone for your specific location with your recruiter.

  • Application Information:
    • Application deadline: applications accepted on an ongoing basis until position is closed and filled
    • This posting is for a new position
  • Reporting to the Engineering Manager, Subscriptions & Payments
As a Frontend Engineer, you’ll…
  • Write performant and accessible User Interfaces that power the Webflow visual design experience
  • Work on technologies such as React,js, Javascript/Typescript, HTML, and CSS
  • Have a strong understanding and expertise in at least one technical domain
  • Contribute to building and executing on functional strategy
  • Scope and implement technical solutions with minimal guidance
  • Produce and elevate the quality of maintainable, tested, performant, and scalable code
  • Collaborate product managers and designers in an autonomous, supportive team environment
  • Participate in engineering citizenship activities such as co-authoring engineering blogs, strengthening and improving our hiring processes, and internal hackathon teams

Requirements

About you:

    • BA/BS degree or equivalent experience
    • Have 2+ years of experience working as a FE/UI engineer with a proven track record of shipping code
    • Are proficient in advanced React and CSS patterns
    • Have a deep, foundational understanding of Javascript and/or Typescript
    • Experience with testing frameworks (e.g. Jest, Mocha, Playwright, Cypress)
    • Treat web accessibility as a top priority
    • Have experience working with feature teams on customer-facing products, shipping and iterating to solve customer problems
    • Value testing and documentation equally as much as your code
    • Are comfortable with ambiguity and scoping solutions with your teammates
    • Have experience working with a fast paced, highly collaborative, and cross-functional team
    • Enjoy high-visibility work and presenting to executive counterparts
    • Are comfortable working in an agile, safe-to-fail environment
    • Stay curious and open to growth — actively building fluency in emerging technologies like AI to unlock creativity, accelerate progress, and amplify impact.
Our Core Behaviors
  • Build lasting customer trust. We build trust by taking action that puts customer trust first.
  • Win together. We play to win, and we win as one team. Success at Webflow isn't a solo act.
  • Reinvent ourselves. We don't just improve what exists, we imagine what's possible.
  • Deliver with speed, quality, and craft. We move fast because the moment demands it, and we do so without lowering the bar.

Benefits
  • Ownership in what you help build. Every permanent Webflower receives equity (RSUs) in our growing, privately held company.
  • Health coverage that actually covers you. Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for full-time employees and their dependents, with Webflow covering most premiums.
  • Support for every stage of family life. 12 weeks of paid parental leave for all parents and 6+ weeks of additional paid leave for birthing parents. Plus inclusive care for family planning, menopause, and midlife transitions.
  • Time off that’s actually off. Flexible vacation, paid holidays, and a sabbatical program to help you recharge and come back inspired.
  • Wellness for the whole you. Access to mental health resources, therapy and coaching.
  • Invest in your future. A 401(k) with 100% employer match (up to $6,000/year) in the U.S., and support for retirement savings globally.
  • Monthly stipends that flex with your life. Localized support for work and wellness expenses — from Wi-Fi to workouts.
  • Bonus for building together. All full-time, permanent, non-commission employees are eligible for our annual WIN bonus program.

Temporary employees may be eligible for paid holiday and time off, statutory leaves of absence, and company-sponsored medical benefits depending on their Fixed Term Contract and their country/state of employment.

Remote, together

At Webflow, equality is a core tenet of our culture. We are an Equal Opportunity (EEO)/Veterans/Disabled Employer and are committed

jobs.title: Front-End Engineer, Subscriptions & Payments
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

At Webflow, we’re building the world’s leading AI-native Digital Experience Platform, and we’re doing it as a remote-first company built on trust, transparency, and a whole lot of creativity. This work takes grit, because we move fast, without ever sacrificing craft or quality. Our mission is to bring development superpowers to everyone. From entrepreneurs launching their first idea to global enterprises scaling their digital presence, we empower teams to design, launch, and optimize for the web without barriers. We believe the future of the web, and work, is more open, more creative, and more equitable. And we’re here to build it together.

We're looking for a Front-End Engineer to join the Subscription & Payments team to define how millions of customers purchase and manage subscriptions. You will design and ship plan selection, checkout, and billing management UIs that are secure, performant, and accessible. Working with product, design, and cross-functional teams, you will influence technical direction, build services to speed up monetization launches, and improve conversion and retention.

About The Role

  • Location: Remote-first (United States; BC & ON, Canada)
  • Full-time
  • Permanent
  • Exempt
  • The cash compensation for this role is tailored to align with the cost of labor in different geographic markets. We've structured the base pay ranges for this role into zones for our geographic markets, and the specific base pay within the range will be determined by the candidate’s geographic location, job-related experience, knowledge, qualifications, and skills.
    • United States (all figures cited below are in USD and pertain to workers in the United States)
      • Zone A: $134,000 - $197,000
      • Zone B: $126,000 - $185,000
      • Zone C: $118,000 - $173,000
    • Canada (figures cited below are in CAD and pertain to workers in ON & BC, Canada)
      • $152,000 - $223,000
This role is also eligible to participate in Webflow's company-wide bonus program. Target amounts are a percentage of base salary and vary by career level. Payouts are based on company performance against established financial and operational goals.

Please visit our Careers page for more information on which locations are included in each of our geographic pay zones. However, please confirm the zone for your specific location with your recruiter.

  • Application Information:
    • Application deadline: applications accepted on an ongoing basis until position is closed and filled
    • This posting is for a new position
  • Reporting to the Engineering Manager, Subscriptions & Payments
As a Frontend Engineer, you’ll…
  • Write performant and accessible User Interfaces that power the Webflow visual design experience
  • Work on technologies such as React,js, Javascript/Typescript, HTML, and CSS
  • Have a strong understanding and expertise in at least one technical domain
  • Contribute to building and executing on functional strategy
  • Scope and implement technical solutions with minimal guidance
  • Produce and elevate the quality of maintainable, tested, performant, and scalable code
  • Collaborate product managers and designers in an autonomous, supportive team environment
  • Participate in engineering citizenship activities such as co-authoring engineering blogs, strengthening and improving our hiring processes, and internal hackathon teams

Requirements

About you:

    • BA/BS degree or equivalent experience
    • Have 2+ years of experience working as a FE/UI engineer with a proven track record of shipping code
    • Are proficient in advanced React and CSS patterns
    • Have a deep, foundational understanding of Javascript and/or Typescript
    • Experience with testing frameworks (e.g. Jest, Mocha, Playwright, Cypress)
    • Treat web accessibility as a top priority
    • Have experience working with feature teams on customer-facing products, shipping and iterating to solve customer problems
    • Value testing and documentation equally as much as your code

    • Enjoy high-visibility work and presenting to executive counterparts

  • Reinvent ourselves. We don't just improve what exists, we imagine what's possible.

Benefits

  • Health coverage that actually covers you. Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for full-time employees and their dependents, with Webflow covering most premiums.

  • Time off that’s actually off. Flexible vacation, paid holidays, and a sabbatical program to help you recharge and come back inspired.

  • Invest in your future. A 401(k) with 100% employer match (up to $6,000/year) in the U.S., and support for retirement savings globally.

jobs.title: Remote Web Developer II
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Position: Web Developer II

Location: Fully Remote

Employment type: Perm

Salary: $110,000 MAX

Clearance: Active Secret Clearance

Certification: Active Sec+


Position Summary

The Web Developer II supports our clients mission to transform federal service delivery. This role reinforces operational excellence, agility, and high-quality delivery across client programs. The Web Developer II is responsible for providing technical expertise, supporting mission-critical delivery, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to advance Snowbird’s objectives.


Primary Responsibilities

- Design and develop user-friendly, accessible web applications.

- Implement APIs, front-end logic, and UI enhancements.

- Ensure Section 508 compliance and federal accessibility standards.

- Debug, test, and optimize web components.

- Work closely with UI/UX and software engineering teams.


Secondary Responsibilities

- Contribute to our clients operational excellence and continuous improvement.

- Support documentation, reporting, and cross-team communication.

- Participate in internal training, coaching, and knowledge-sharing activities.


Required Clearance and Certification

Active T3 – DoD/DHA SECRET

Active Security+ Certification


Desired Technical Skillset

- Microsoft SiteCore

- C# (.NET Core)


Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.

- 6 years of relevant professional experience.

- Strong communication and collaboration skills.

- Federal contracting experience preferred.


Compliance Requirements

- Must pass federal background checks.

- Maintain annual cybersecurity and ethics training.

- Adhere to our clients and federal guidelines.

jobs.title: Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Our Purpose:

At Gardyn, we’re on a mission to make fresh, healthy food accessible to every home. We believe growing food should be simple, sustainable, and joyful and we’re building smart indoor gardening technology that helps people eat better while using fewer natural resources.


We’re a fast-growing, mission-driven company that cares deeply about impact, innovation, and how we show up for one another. Our work is rooted in curiosity, ownership, and thoughtful growth and we’re excited to bring on leaders who want to help scale something meaningful.


The Opportunity:

As our Web Developer, you’ll play a key role in shaping and scaling Gardyn’s Shopify experience. Our website is more than a storefront, it’s where customers first connect with our brand, understand our technology, and decide to grow with us. You’ll help ensure that experience is fast, polished, and technically sound, supporting both day-to-day execution and larger growth initiatives.


In 2026, you’ll be instrumental in supporting our WooCommerce to Shopify migration, helping build a scalable, high-performing foundation for the future. Working closely with Creative, Growth, and the Web team, you’ll bring campaigns, experiments, and optimizations to life with clean, reliable code that improves performance, conversion, and overall user experience.


What You’ll Do:

High-Quality, Reliable Shopify Development
  • Build, refine, and maintain Shopify templates, front-end components, styles, and theme functionality using clean, reusable, well-documented code.
  • Support the WooCommerce to Shopify migration by translating existing templates, implementing new theme structures, and ensuring parity and brand consistency across the redesigned experience.
  • Ensure cross-browser and cross-device accuracy through structured QA, debugging, and proactive refinement.

Improve Web Performance & Conversion
  • Improve site performance by optimizing code, reducing DOM complexity, compressing assets, eliminating unused scripts and styles, and following Shopify performance best practices.
  • Partner with Web and Growth to implement A/B tests and CRO-driven UI variations with clean experiment code that preserves measurement integrity and supports rapid iteration.
  • Embed accessibility and responsive design best practices across all pages and components to deliver high-quality, inclusive experiences.

Strong Collaboration & Cross-Functional Execution
  • Collaborate closely with the Head of Website, Creative, Growth, and the Web Dev Lead to translate UX and design requirements into high-quality production code.
  • Contribute technical recommendations during scoping, offering solutions that balance quality, feasibility, scalability, and timelines.
  • Assist with issue resolution by monitoring site behavior, responding to reported bugs, and maintaining documentation for components, workflows, and updates.


What We’re Looking For:

Shopify & Front-End Expertise
  • Strong proficiency in Shopify theme development using Liquid, including building and extending reference themes (e.g., Dawn).
  • Highly proficient in HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript (ES6+), with experience in modern CSS architecture (Sass/SCSS, CSS variables, structured methodologies).
  • Experience integrating Shopify apps, working with metafields and structured data models, and supporting store operations.
  • Familiarity with modern build tools and Git-based version control workflows.

Performance, Optimization & Quality
  • Deep understanding of responsive design, cross-browser testing, accessibility best practices (WCAG, semantic markup), and performance optimization.
  • Experience implementing A/B tests and supporting CRO initiatives with clean, experiment-ready code.
  • Strong QA and debugging skills with attention to detail and production reliability.

Technical Breadth & Collaboration
  • Experience consuming REST APIs and integrating third-party services.
  • Familiarity with analytics and marketing tools (GA, GTM, ContentSquare, custom event tracking).
  • Strategic thinker who is equally comfortable executing at a high level of detail.
  • Collaborative, low-ego partner with strong ownership and accountability.


Why You'll Love This Role:
  • We encourage unconventional thinking.
  • We are paving the path for innovation in home-grown food and education.
  • We are creating a movement bigger than ourselves that will positively enhance people’s lives.
  • We practice radical candor and encourage employees to be authentic and transparent when communicating with all internal employees as well as external vendor relationships.
  • We lead with context not control and let you determine the best ways to accomplish your goals.
  • We are a wicked smart yet humble team.
  • Gardyn embodies the start-up culture with the success to back it up.


We provide competitive and comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings designed to support you at work and in life. These include: medical, dental, and vision coverage, employer paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, life insurance, a 401(k) with company matching, unlimited paid time off, paid company holidays, Take Care Fridays during the summer, a fully remote work environment, education reimbursement, travel reimbursement, a Gardyn device and membership, and exceptional paid maternity and paternity leave options. The pay range for this role is $85,000 – $100,000 per year, based on experience, skills, and market alignment.

jobs.title: Lead Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Lead Web Developer (Webflow)

Marketing Websites | UX + Implementation


We are partnering with a growing organization seeking a Lead Web Developer to support their corporate marketing team responsible for building and maintaining high-impact marketing web experiences.
  • This is a hands-on Webflow production role focused on creating, updating, and optimizing marketing websites and landing pages. The role sits within Marketing and works closely with creative and campaign teams to turn design direction into fully functional pages.
  • This is not a product engineering or enterprise application development role. Instead, this position focuses on building and managing marketing web pages directly in Webflow while balancing design thinking and implementation.
  • The role typically operates in a blended capacity of design and development, roughly 50 percent page building and development and 50 percent layout, UX, and visual implementation.


What You Will Do
  • Build and publish marketing pages directly in Webflow including landing pages, campaign pages, and website updates.
  • Own Webflow CMS collections, templates, and page structure.
  • Translate creative direction and campaign requirements into fully functional Webflow builds.
  • Create page layouts and user flows when design direction is not fully defined.
  • Refine and adjust design directly inside Webflow.
  • Implement responsive pages across desktop, tablet, and mobile.
  • Write and implement HTML, CSS, and JavaScript within Webflow when needed.
  • Set up forms, tracking scripts, and third-party integrations.
  • Optimize pages for SEO, performance, accessibility, and conversion.
  • Build landing pages quickly to support marketing campaigns.
  • Implement micro-interactions and animations when needed.
  • Collaborate with marketing, creative, and digital teams.


What We Are Looking For
  • Hands-on experience building websites directly in Webflow.
  • Experience creating and managing Webflow CMS collections and page templates.
  • Ability to build complete marketing pages in Webflow from scratch.
  • Comfort working with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript within Webflow environments.
  • Strong understanding of UX fundamentals, layout hierarchy, and conversion-focused design.
  • Ability to translate design direction into functional web pages.
  • Experience working in marketing, campaign, or growth-focused environments.
  • Comfort working in fast-paced environments where landing pages and campaigns move quickly.


Nice to Have
  • API integrations or light backend exposure.
  • Experience connecting websites with marketing automation or CRM platforms.
  • Experience working with distributed teams or global stakeholders.


Tools Commonly Used
  • Webflow CMS
  • Figma or similar design tools
  • Analytics and tracking tools
  • SEO optimization tools


Who This Role Is For
  • A mid-level Webflow builder who enjoys working at the intersection of design and development.
  • Someone who is comfortable creating marketing pages quickly while maintaining strong UX and design quality.
  • A web professional who enjoys working closely with marketing teams and contributing directly to campaign performance.

jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Our mission is to improve patient outcomes by helping healthcare organizations reduce costs and operate more efficiently. We deliver value through innovative cost-management solutions, detailed transaction analysis, and enhanced visibility into operational processes. By partnering with our clients, we help them better understand and manage their cost cycles—enabling them to control expenses, mitigate risk, and redirect resources toward patient care.


We are seeking a Full Stack Developer to contribute to the development and growth of a cloud-based SaaS platform focused on pharmacy procurement and analytics. This role is well suited for someone who enjoys building high-quality software products that create meaningful value for customers through intuitive user experiences and reliable, scalable systems.

As part of a collaborative product team, you will work across the full technology stack—contributing to front-end interfaces, back-end services, cloud infrastructure, and supporting systems. You will also help influence the technical direction of the platform while delivering new features that support customer success.


Responsibilities
  • Design, develop, and maintain full-stack web applications within a cloud-native SaaS environment
  • Build responsive and intuitive front-end interfaces using modern frameworks such as React, Vue, or Angular
  • Integrate external systems and APIs to support internal workflows and customer integrations
  • Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested code with appropriate documentation
  • Collaborate closely with product managers and designers to translate business requirements into technical solutions
  • Partner with IT and infrastructure teams to manage and monitor the cloud environment
  • Design, optimize, and maintain data models and databases to ensure performance and reliability
  • Contribute to CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices to support efficient and reliable deployments
  • Monitor applications in production and troubleshoot issues across the full stack when needed
  • Stay up to date with emerging technologies, tools, and best practices in SaaS development


Desired Education and Experience
  • 5+ years of experience as a Full Stack Developer or in a similar role within a SaaS environment
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
  • Experience working in regulated industries such as healthcare, finance, or pharmacy is preferred
  • Strong back-end development experience using modern frameworks such as Django, .NET, Spring, or similar technologies
  • Proficiency with JavaScript or TypeScript and modern front-end frameworks including React, Vue, or Angular
  • Experience designing and integrating RESTful APIs and connecting with external services
  • Working knowledge of cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud, including core services related to compute, storage, networking, and databases
  • Experience with relational and/or NoSQL databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, MongoDB, or DynamoDB
  • Familiarity with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization technologies such as Docker and/or Kubernetes is preferred
  • Understanding of security best practices including authentication, authorization, and data protection
  • Ability to work effectively in Agile environments and collaborate with cross-functional teams across multiple locations and time zones
  • Strong problem-solving, communication, and organizational skills

  • Proficiency with JavaScript or TypeScript and modern front-end frameworks including React, Vue, or Angular
  • Experience designing and integrating RESTful APIs and connecting with external services
  • Working knowledge of cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud, including core services related to compute, storage, networking, and databases
  • Experience with relational and/or NoSQL databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, MongoDB, or DynamoDB
  • Familiarity with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization technologies such as Docker and/or Kubernetes is preferred
  • Understanding of security best practices including authentication, authorization, and data protection
  • Ability to work effectively in Agile environments and collaborate with cross-functional teams across multiple locations and time zones
  • Strong problem-solving, communication, and organizational skills

jobs.title: Senior Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

We believe communication belongs to everyone. We exist to democratize phone service.  TextNow is evolving the way the world connects and that's because we're made up of people with curious minds who bring an optimistic, yet critical lens into the work we do.   We're the largest provider of free phone service in the nation. And we're just getting started.

Join us in our mission to break down barriers to communication and free the flow of conversation for people everywhere.

TextNow is looking for an experienced Senior Web Developer to play a key role in the development of our voice and messaging platform on Web. You will be responsible for building high-performance, scalable, and feature-rich mobile applications that integrate voice, video, and messaging capabilities. You will work alongside a talented team of developers, product managers, and UX/UI designers to create intuitive and user-centric experiences.


What You'll Do
  • Design, develop, and maintain a high-quality Web application focused on voice and messaging features (voice calling, chat, real-time notifications, etc.).
  • Write clean, maintainable and testable Web applications in Javascript using the latest programming techniques and frameworks
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and ship new features.
  • Work with real-time communication protocols, such as WebRTC, SIP, and related messaging and voice frameworks.
  • Optimize app performance to ensure smooth voice and messaging functionality, including audio quality, latency, and bandwidth usage.
  • Troubleshoot and debug issues related to performance, crashes, and other bugs, ensuring a smooth and stable user experience.
  • Own problems and solutions end-to-end, from ideation to deployment
  • Experimentation is a key part of your workflow; be data-driven and make the right decisions for our customers
  • Deploy your features to millions of users - your work will have an immediate impact.


Who You Are
  • A professional Web developer with 5+ years of experience with web app development.
  • Experience in Javascript, HTML and CSS, with a deep understanding of web frameworks (React, Marionette).
  • Bonus points for experience with real-time communication protocols and technologies like WebRTC, SIP, or similar.
  • Knowledge of desktop notifications and managing network connections for real-time updates.
  • Familiarity with modern web development tools, Instruments, and the debugging process.
  • Understanding of user experience (UX) principles, with the ability to design elegant, simple solutions for complex problems.
  • Experience with automated testing frameworks (Unit, UI, Integration testing).



More about TextNow


Our culture

We’re proud of the culture that we’ve built at TextNow, but one of the most common questions we hear is ‘how do you continue to sustain it as the world and the company continues to change?’ The reality is that we’re only able to keep up because each and every TextNovian contributes to our culture through being involved, by living our values, sharing feedback, embracing change and more!

Our values

Customer Obsessed

We strive to have a deep understanding of our customers.


Do Right By Our People

We treat each other with fairness, respect, and integrity.


Accept the Challenge

We adopt a "Yes, We Can" mindset to achieve ambitious goals.


Act Like an Owner

We treat this company like it's our own... because it is!


Give a Damn

We are deeply committed and passionate about our work and achieving results.


Our benefits and more

This is a brief overview of the benefits that TextNow offers its employees. More complete details can be found in TextNow’s Benefit Guide and legal plan documents, which are available to employees on or shortly after their start date with TextNow. The benefits listed herein are for illustrative purposes only and may change from time-to-time in TextNow’s sole discretion.

Free phone service

Strong work life blend

Flexible work arrangements (work-from-home, remote, or access to one of our of

jobs.title: Hydrogen/React Front End Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

To ensure a fair and efficient hiring process, candidates should not contact UN/COMMON employees directly regarding this role. Applications and communication must occur through the official application process.

You're a senior frontend technologist who thrives in modern headless ecommerce environments and enjoys building scalable, high-performance storefront experiences using Shopify Hydrogen, React, and TypeScript. You're comfortable working within complex enterprise architectures and collaborating closely with designers, project managers, and backend engineers to deliver reliable, production-ready solutions.

You enjoy solving real-world commerce challenges such as inventory rendering, performance optimization, and complex UI logic, and you're confident working inside evolving codebases. You communicate clearly, move efficiently within sprint workflows, and help elevate frontend architecture standards across projects.

This role will primarily support ongoing development for an enterprise storefront leveraging Hydrogen for core customer-facing experiences.

  • Develop and maintain Hydrogen-based storefront features using React and TypeScript
  • Build scalable, reusable frontend components aligned with modern React patterns
  • Collaborate with internal delivery teams to implement new UX features and enhancements
  • Translate design concepts and technical requirements into performant storefront experiences
  • Work within existing Hydrogen repositories and enterprise Shopify architecture
  • Optimize rendering performance, state management, and data fetching strategies
  • Integrate APIs and backend services supporting product, inventory, and checkout workflows
  • Troubleshoot complex frontend issues across devices, browsers, and environments
  • Participate in code reviews and maintain strong Git version control practices
  • Document technical decisions and support ongoing maintainability of the codebase

Requirements
  • 4+ years of frontend development experience with strong React fundamentals
  • Demonstrated experience working with Shopify Hydrogen storefronts in production
  • Advanced proficiency in TypeScript, React, modern JavaScript (ES6+)
  • Experience with headless ecommerce or composable storefront architectures
  • Strong understanding of GraphQL data fetching patterns and API integrations
  • Comfort working within enterprise-level repositories and collaborative development workflows
  • Experience optimizing performance, accessibility, and frontend architecture
  • Ability to work independently while communicating clearly with cross-functional teams
  • Familiarity with Shopify ecosystem concepts such as Storefront API, metafields, and custom logic is strongly preferred

Nice to Have
  • Experience with Node.js middleware or hybrid Shopify architectures
  • Previous work on enterprise retail or high-traffic ecommerce storefronts
  • Experience with design systems and component libraries
  • Exposure to AEM or other CMS integrations

Benefits

Hourly Rate: $30-$40 USD per hour depending on experience

Weekly Hours: Approximately 20-30 hours per week

Project Duration: March 2026 through August 1st, 2026 with potential extension

Engagement Type: Contract / Freelance

jobs.title: Senior Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

An ongoing client of ours at Talent, is hiring for a Senior Web Developer to join their team. Our client is a leading provider in the healthcare industry for patient care. You will be responsible to help the development team design, build, maintain modern web-based applications.


You will work with a modern Microsoft technology stack including .NET, C#, Angular, Azure and AI-assisted development tools.


Fully Remote - Must be EST time zone.

6 month contract to start


Responsibilities:

  • Design, develop, and deploy web-based applications
  • Write clean and efficient code using C#, ASP.NET Core, Angular, and Entity Framework Core
  • Build RESTful APIs
  • Work with Azure DevOps to manage user stories, version control and deployment pipelines
  • Utilize AI-assisted development tools (Copilot or similar AI coding assistants) to support coding, debugging, and development workflows


Required Experience:

  • 5+ years of professional software development experience
  • Strong experience with C#, .NET/ ASP.NET Core and Angular
  • Experience leveraging AI-assisted development tools - preferred Copilot
  • Experience building web applications using Entity Framework Core and Web APIs
  • Experience working with JavaScript and Typescript


This role is for someone who can work independently while collaborating with a team and is a passionate developer about writing clean and scalable code.


Please apply directly or send over an email to jillian.pepe@talentinternational.com. Interviews are early as March 9th!


**Please no 3rd party reach out**

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Full-Stack
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

About Verdent：

Verdent AI is an early-stage company set to redefine the software development lifecycle. Our mission is to build an AI-powered coding partner that acts as a seamless extension of the developer's mind.We have created a tool that doesn't just suggest code—it understands intent, anticipates needs, and automates entire workflows.

Our founding team brings experience from the world's leading AI labs and enterprise software companies. We are funded, focused, and looking for exceptional builders to join our core team and help us scale a product that developers already love and rely on.


About the Role：

As a Founding Engineer, you will be a pillar of our company. You will have outsized ownership and impact, joining us at a pivotal moment to iterate on and expand our live product. This role demands deep technical expertise, strong product intuition, and the ability to navigate ambiguity.You will make foundational decisions that will shape our architecture, our product, and our engineering culture for years to come.


Responsibilities:
  • Design, build, and deploy robust, high-performance backend services and infrastructure that power our core AI capabilities.
  • Develop intuitive, responsive, and polished user interfaces across the full stack, designed to integrate seamlessly into developer workflows.
  • Architect and implement sophisticated Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems to provide highly contextual and accurate code assistance.
  • Integrate, evaluate, and productionize state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs) to power our core code generation, debugging, and chat features.
  • Move with velocity: Rapidly prototype, ship, and iterate on new features based on direct feedback from our active user base.
  • Take full ownership of systems from the database to the client-side, ensuring they are scalable, reliable, and secure.
  • Obsess over the developer experience, latency, and performance of every feature you build.


Nice to have:

  • 3+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a proven track record of shipping high-quality, user-facing products.
  • Experience building IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains).
  • A deep understanding of static analysis, compilers, or abstract syntax trees (ASTs).
  • Experience with model fine-tuning and evaluation.
  • Active participation in open-source developer communities.
  • Prior experience in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment.


2Annual Salary

$250,000 - $450,000 USD

jobs.title: Frontend Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

About Elligint Health

Elligint Health, established in 2024, is leading the charge of innovating healthcare by aligning all stakeholders, delivering intelligent healthcare solutions, and empowering pro-active, whole-person care across the healthcare continuum. Elligint Health integrates vast amounts of data from across the healthcare continuum, delivering intelligence that informs decision-making, enhances care coordination, and improves outcomes. Focused on enabling actionable intervention and whole-person care, Elligint Health helps healthcare organizations navigate complexity, turning insights into strategies that benefit providers, payers, and members and patients alike. With Elligint Health, the future of healthcare is simpler, smarter, and more effective.

The Position

We are looking for an experienced Frontend Developer to join our team. You will work on complex web applications (SPA), refactor legacy code, and help shape the future of our product. This role requires strong expertise in Vue.js, React, and TypeScript, as well as the ability to design scalable, maintainable solutions.

Duties & Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain complex SPA applications
  • Refactor and gradually migrate legacy code (e.g., from Vue 2.x to Vue 3)
  • Build and maintain reusable component libraries and design systems
  • Ensure responsive, accessible, and user-friendly UI/UX
  • Integrate with REST APIs and WebSocket real-time communication
  • Write and maintain unit/integration tests
  • Collaborate with backend developers, product managers, and designers
  • Participate in code reviews and contribute to improving team practices

Required Qualifications
  • JavaScript / TypeScript – strong ES6+ and TypeScript knowledge, modules, async, performance, and security best practices
  • Vue.js – solid experience with 2.6/2.7 and 3, including Composition API
  • React.js – hands-on experience with Hooks and Context API
  • jQuery – ability to support and gradually remove legacy code
  • HTML5 / CSS3 – responsive design, animations, cross-browser compatibility, SCSS/PostCSS
  • API integration – REST, JSON, WebSockets, OpenAPI 3.0 (Swagger/Redoc)
  • Build tools – Webpack 5, Vite, Rollup, Babel, ESbuild, ESLint, Prettier, Stylelint
  • Testing – Jest, Mocha, or equivalent frameworks
  • Git – confident use of Git workflows (branching, code reviews, CI)
  • Strong communication and problem-solving skills
  • Proactive team player that is eager to learn

Preferred Qualifications
  • Backend basics (PHP, MySQL)
  • Experience with component libraries and design systems
  • DevOps basics (CI/CD, Docker, deployment, monitoring)
  • Security knowledge (JWT, OAuth2 scopes, API keys)

Compensation
  • Competitive salary
  • PTO and company sick leave
  • Rapidly growing technology company with upside potential

Elligint Health is committed to ensuring that information security remains a top priority for everyone. All workers are responsible for the protection of our Information Security, and we take the execution of this seriously. Information Security Policies and procedures details and training will be provided during onboarding.

Each candidate will be subject to a drug screening, background check, and reference check before beginning employment. Please note that some of our positions require U.S. citizenship and submission and further approval of “Public Trust” federal clearance.

jobs.title: Web Developer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Location: Remote
Department: IT / Digital Development
Reports To: IT Manager / Director of Technology
Employment Type: Full-Time / Contract

Position Overview

We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Web Developer to design, develop, and maintain high-performing websites and web applications in a fully remote environment. The ideal candidate will have strong technical expertise, a creative problem-solving mindset, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver responsive and user-friendly digital experiences.

This role requires strong coding skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage projects independently.

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain company websites and web applications

Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code

Collaborate with designers, content creators, and marketing teams

Optimize websites for speed, performance, and scalability

Ensure cross-browser compatibility and mobile responsiveness

Implement security best practices and data protection measures

Troubleshoot and debug issues promptly

Integrate third-party APIs and tools

Conduct website updates, backups, and ongoing maintenance

Stay current with emerging web technologies and industry trends

Qualifications & Requirements

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Web Development, or related field (or equivalent experience)

2+ years of professional web development experience

Proficiency in HTML, CSS, JavaScript

Experience with frameworks such as React, Angular, or Vue.js

Experience with backend technologies (Node.js, PHP, Python, or similar)

Familiarity with CMS platforms (WordPress, Shopify, or similar)

Knowledge of databases (MySQL, MongoDB, etc.)

Understanding of SEO principles and web performance optimization

Strong problem-solving and communication skills

Ability to work independently in a remote setting

Preferred Skills

Experience with cloud hosting platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

Knowledge of version control systems (Git)

Experience with CI/CD pipelines

UI/UX design understanding

E-commerce development experience

Work Environment

Fully remote

Flexible working hours (as applicable)

Project-based and deadline-driven environment

Compensation & Benefits

Competitive salary or contract rate

Health, dental, and vision insurance (if applicable)

Paid time off

Professional development opportunities

Remote work flexibility

jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

Full Stack Engineer (Front-End Focus)


At Pienso, we're not just building another tech platform; we're creating a suite of tools that will revolutionize the way businesses leverage language AI.


Our mission is to provide tailored and robust Large Language Model (LLM) tools that seamlessly integrate advanced AI technology with the unique needs of each enterprise.


We're aiming to become the go-to tool for business analysts.


Our platform offers unmatched functionality, flexibility, and user experience, empowering enterprise users to easily extract insights from complex text data sets.


Why join Pienso?


At Pienso, you'll have the opportunity to:
  • Work on cutting-edge technology that combines AI and business intelligence.
  • Tackle meaningful and challenging projects with significant industry impact.
  • Join a passionate team that values innovation, creativity, and personal growth.
  • Influence the future of machine learning applications across diverse industries.


Your Role at Pienso

We are seeking a highly skilled Full Stack Engineer with a strong front-end focus to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will both drive the development of our custom Angular component library and play a key role in building new features across our platform. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including UX/UI designers, product managers, and backend engineers, to deliver a cohesive, scalable, and high-quality user experience.


Key Responsibilities
  • Design, implement, and maintain a scalable, reusable Angular component library that meets the needs of our platform and delights end-users.
  • Lead the integration of the component library across the platform application, ensuring consistency, accessibility, and performance.
  • Build new full-stack platform features end-to-end, from frontend interfaces to backend APIs and data layers.
  • Collaborate with UX/UI designers to translate designs into robust, production-ready components and features, and ensure technical feasibility.
  • Establish and enforce front-end coding standards, component architecture patterns, and documentation practices for the library.
  • Participate in code reviews, contributing to the improvement of internal standards and ensuring component quality and reusability.
  • Prototype and iterate on components and platform features based on feedback from stakeholders and user testing.
  • Ensure the scalability and performance of applications through effective architecture and caching strategies.
  • Stay up-to-date with emerging trends in Angular, front-end architecture, component design systems, and full-stack development.


Requirements
  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
  • Strong proficiency in TypeScript and Angular, with demonstrated experience building or consuming component libraries or design systems.
  • Solid understanding of Angular architecture including modules, services, directives, and change detection strategies.
  • Proficiency with Node.js or similar backend technologies, with experience designing and building production APIs and services.
  • Familiarity with database technologies, both SQL (e.g., PostgreSQL) and alternatives.
  • Experience with CSS architecture and design token systems to support consistent, themeable component styling.
  • Familiarity with accessibility standards (WCAG) and their implementation in reusable UI components.
  • Familiarity with RESTful API design and development.
  • Experience with cloud services (AWS, Google Cloud, or Azure) and modern deployment practices.
  • Experience with Docker or similar container technologies.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a fast-paced, evolving environment. Prior experience in a startup environment.
  • Contributions to open-source projects, particularly front-end or UI-related, are a plus.


Benefits
  • Competitive salary and equity package.
  • Flexible working hours and remote work opportunities.
  • Generous vacation and leave policies.
  • Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.
  • A vibrant team culture with regular team outings and events.
  • Health, dental and vision coverage, including HDHP with HSA options
  • Company-paid basic life/AD&D insurance
  • Monthly wellness allowance
  • 401k plan, with pre-tax or Roth IRA options


How to Apply


Send your CV to careers@pienso.com for immediate consideration.


Ready to Make a Difference?

Apply now to join our team and help shape the future of machine learning.


We look forward to hearing from you!

jobs.title: Frontend Engineer - RealTime 3D
jobs.description:
Sobre a vaga

The Role

We build desktop-class analytical systems that run inside the browser. You'll shape the evolution of our realtime 3D visualization stack: rendering, interaction, and performance for extremely large realtime datasets. 3D graphs with hundreds of thousands of nodes, nonlinear video-style timelines, and interactive data visualizations in many forms. This work is artistic, performance-sensitive, and architecturally nuanced. We expect you to use AI tools to move fast, and to own what you ship.


What You'll Do
  • Build and optimize systems that render hundreds of thousands of nodes in real time
  • Push WebGL toward desktop-class performance through profiling, benchmarking, and iteration
  • Build the foundational rendering vocabulary and tools that other team engineers reach for
  • Use AI tooling to accelerate experimentation and shader iteration, without giving up architectural control
  • Daily standups, pair programming sessions, and code reviews with our creative and talented frontend team


Who We're Looking For
  • Senior-level frontend experience with TypeScript, React, Three.js, WebGL, and R3F
  • Bonus points for shader experience (GLSL/WGSL), WebGPU, and spatial data structures
  • Strong intuition for performance, memory behavior, and browser rendering constraints
  • You've built serious 3D or graphics-intensive web applications
  • You're comfortable owning complex systems, not just contributing to one
  • You communicate clearly, written and spoken
  • 2+ years working effectively in remote environments
  • Personal portfolio demos that make people ask "wait, this runs in a browser?"


Strong Signals
  • Background in linear algebra, computational geometry, or graph layouts
  • Prior work on visualization engines or custom rendering pipelines
  • Active or willing to obtain a Top Secret clearance
